 * Dependencies: Joomla 5.1.2, backtest and attribution modules
 * =====================================================================
 */



/**
 * Generate evaluation report for a game
 * 
 * @param string $gameId           Game identifier
 * @param string $periodStart      Evaluation period start date (YYYY-MM-DD)
 * @param string $periodEnd        Evaluation period end date (YYYY-MM-DD)
 * @param array  $beforeAfter      Optional: before/after comparison data
 * @return array                   Report data
 */
function SKAI_generateEvaluationReport($gameId, $periodStart, $periodEnd, $beforeAfter = null) {
    try {
        // Get backtest metrics for period
        $metrics = SKAI_getBacktestMetricsForPeriod($gameId, $periodStart, $periodEnd);
        
        if (empty($metrics)) {
            return [
                'error' => 'No backtest metrics found for this period',
                'game_id' => $gameId,
                'period_start' => $periodStart,
                'period_end' => $periodEnd
            ];
        }
        
        // Calculate aggregate metrics
        $numDraws = count($metrics);
        $totalRecall = 0.0;
        $totalNDCG = 0.0;
        $totalMWR = 0.0;
        $totalT5 = 0.0;
        $totalT10 = 0.0;
        $totalMRRW = 0.0;
        $totalLift = 0.0;
        $totalStable = 0.0;
        $totalEliteJ = 0.0;
        
        foreach ($metrics as $m) {
            $totalRecall += $m['recall_at_20'];
            $totalNDCG += $m['ndcg_at_20'];
            $totalMWR += $m['mean_winner_rank'];
            $totalT5 += (float)($m['top5_containment_rate'] ?? 0.0);
            $totalT10 += (float)($m['top10_containment_rate'] ?? 0.0);
            $totalMRRW += (float)($m['mean_reciprocal_winner_rank'] ?? 0.0);
            $totalLift += (
                (float)($m['winner_rank_lift_vs_random'] ?? 0.0) +
                (float)($m['winner_rank_lift_vs_frequency'] ?? 0.0) +
                (float)($m['winner_rank_lift_vs_recency'] ?? 0.0) +
                (float)($m['winner_rank_lift_vs_skip'] ?? 0.0)
            ) / 4.0;
            $totalStable += (float)($m['rolling_stability_score'] ?? 0.0);
            $totalEliteJ += (float)($m['elite_objective_j'] ?? 0.0);
        }
        
        $avgRecall = $numDraws > 0 ? $totalRecall / $numDraws : 0.0;
        $avgNDCG = $numDraws > 0 ? $totalNDCG / $numDraws : 0.0;
        $avgMWR = $numDraws > 0 ? $totalMWR / $numDraws : 0.0;
        $avgT5 = $numDraws > 0 ? $totalT5 / $numDraws : 0.0;
        $avgT10 = $numDraws > 0 ? $totalT10 / $numDraws : 0.0;
        $avgMRRW = $numDraws > 0 ? $totalMRRW / $numDraws : 0.0;
        $avgLift = $numDraws > 0 ? $totalLift / $numDraws : 0.0;
        $avgStable = $numDraws > 0 ? $totalStable / $numDraws : 0.0;
        $avgEliteJ = $numDraws > 0 ? $totalEliteJ / $numDraws : 0.0;
        
        // Get top miss reasons
        $topMissReasons = SKAI_getTopMissReasons($gameId, 10);
        
        // Build summary text
        $summaryText = SKAI_buildReportSummary($gameId, $avgRecall, $avgNDCG, $avgMWR, $numDraws, $topMissReasons);
        
        // Store report in database
        $reportId = SKAI_storeEvaluationReport(
            $gameId,
            $periodStart,
            $periodEnd,
            $numDraws,
            $avgRecall,
            $avgNDCG,
            $avgMWR,
            $topMissReasons,
            $beforeAfter,
            $summaryText
        );
        
        return [
            'report_id' => $reportId,
            'game_id' => $gameId,
            'period_start' => $periodStart,
            'period_end' => $periodEnd,
            'num_draws_evaluated' => $numDraws,
            'avg_recall_at_20' => $avgRecall,
            'avg_ndcg_at_20' => $avgNDCG,
            'avg_mean_winner_rank' => $avgMWR,
            'top5_containment_rate' => $avgT5,
            'top10_containment_rate' => $avgT10,
            'mean_reciprocal_winner_rank' => $avgMRRW,
            'avg_rank_lift' => $avgLift,
            'rolling_stability_score' => $avgStable,
            'elite_objective_j' => $avgEliteJ,
            'top_miss_reasons' => $topMissReasons,
            'before_after' => $beforeAfter,
            'summary_text' => $summaryText
        ];
        
    } catch (\Exception $e) {
        error_log('[SKAI] generateEvaluationReport error: ' . $e->getMessage());
        return ['error' => $e->getMessage()];
    }
}

/**
 * Get backtest metrics for a date period
 */
function SKAI_getBacktestMetricsForPeriod($gameId, $periodStart, $periodEnd) {
    try {
        $db = Factory::getDbo();
        $query = $db->getQuery(true);
        
        $query->select('*')
            ->from($db->quoteName('#__skai_backtest_metrics'))
            ->where($db->quoteName('game_id') . ' = ' . $db->quote($gameId))
            ->where($db->quoteName('test_draw_date') . ' >= ' . $db->quote($periodStart))
            ->where($db->quoteName('test_draw_date') . ' <= ' . $db->quote($periodEnd))
            ->order($db->quoteName('test_draw_date') . ' ASC');
        
        $db->setQuery($query);
        return $db->loadAssocList();
        
    } catch (\Exception $e) {
        error_log('[SKAI] getBacktestMetricsForPeriod error: ' . $e->getMessage());
        return [];
    }
}

/**
 * Build human-readable report summary
 */
function SKAI_buildReportSummary($gameId, $avgRecall, $avgNDCG, $avgMWR, $numDraws, $topMissReasons) {
    $summary = "SKAI Evaluation Report - {$gameId}\n";
    $summary .= "=========================================\n\n";
    
    $summary .= "Performance Metrics (Last {$numDraws} draws):\n";
    $summary .= "  - Average Recall@20: " . number_format($avgRecall * 100, 2) . "%\n";
    $summary .= "  - Average NDCG@20: " . number_format($avgNDCG, 4) . "\n";
    $summary .= "  - Average Winner Rank: " . number_format($avgMWR, 2) . "\n\n";
    
    $summary .= "Top Miss Reasons:\n";
    foreach ($topMissReasons as $idx => $reason) {
        $summary .= "  " . ($idx + 1) . ". " . $reason['reason'] . ": " . $reason['count'] . " occurrences (" . number_format($reason['pct'], 1) . "%)\n";
    }
    
    $summary .= "\nBuilt on probabilities--not promises.\n";
    
    return $summary;
}

/**
 * Store evaluation report in database
 */
function SKAI_storeEvaluationReport($gameId, $periodStart, $periodEnd, $numDraws, $avgRecall, $avgNDCG, $avgMWR, $topMissReasons, $beforeAfter, $summaryText) {
    try {
        $db = Factory::getDbo();
        
        // Check if report for this game/date already exists
        $query = $db->getQuery(true);
        $reportDate = new Date('now');
        $reportDateStr = $reportDate->format('Y-m-d');
        
        $query->select('id')
            ->from($db->quoteName('#__skai_evaluation_reports'))
            ->where($db->quoteName('game_id') . ' = ' . $db->quote($gameId))
            ->where($db->quoteName('report_date') . ' = ' . $db->quote($reportDateStr));
        
        $db->setQuery($query);
        $existingId = $db->loadResult();
        
        if ($existingId) {
            // Update existing report
            $query = $db->getQuery(true);
            $query->update($db->quoteName('#__skai_evaluation_reports'))
                ->set($db->quoteName('evaluation_period_start') . ' = ' . $db->quote($periodStart))
                ->set($db->quoteName('evaluation_period_end') . ' = ' . $db->quote($periodEnd))
                ->set($db->quoteName('num_draws_evaluated') . ' = ' . (int)$numDraws)
                ->set($db->quoteName('avg_recall_at_20') . ' = ' . (float)$avgRecall)
                ->set($db->quoteName('avg_ndcg_at_20') . ' = ' . (float)$avgNDCG)
                ->set($db->quoteName('avg_mean_winner_rank') . ' = ' . (float)$avgMWR)
                ->set($db->quoteName('top_miss_reasons_json') . ' = ' . $db->quote(json_encode($topMissReasons)))
                ->set($db->quoteName('before_after_json') . ' = ' . ($beforeAfter ? $db->quote(json_encode($beforeAfter)) : 'NULL'))
                ->set($db->quoteName('summary_text') . ' = ' . $db->quote($summaryText))
                ->where($db->quoteName('id') . ' = ' . (int)$existingId);
            
            $db->setQuery($query);
            $db->execute();
            
            return $existingId;
        } else {
            // Insert new report
            $columns = [
                'game_id',
                'report_date',
                'evaluation_period_start',
                'evaluation_period_end',
                'num_draws_evaluated',
                'avg_recall_at_20',
                'avg_ndcg_at_20',
                'avg_mean_winner_rank',
                'top_miss_reasons_json',
                'before_after_json',
                'summary_text',
                'created_at'
            ];
            
            $now = new Date('now');
            $values = [
                $db->quote($gameId),
                $db->quote($reportDateStr),
                $db->quote($periodStart),
                $db->quote($periodEnd),
                (int)$numDraws,
                (float)$avgRecall,
                (float)$avgNDCG,
                (float)$avgMWR,
                $db->quote(json_encode($topMissReasons)),
                $beforeAfter ? $db->quote(json_encode($beforeAfter)) : 'NULL',
                $db->quote($summaryText),
                $db->quote($now->toSql())
            ];
            
            $query = $db->getQuery(true);
            $query->insert($db->quoteName('#__skai_evaluation_reports'))
                ->columns($db->quoteName($columns))
                ->values(implode(',', $values));
            
            $db->setQuery($query);
            $db->execute();
            
            return $db->insertid();
        }
        
    } catch (\Exception $e) {
        error_log('[SKAI] storeEvaluationReport error: ' . $e->getMessage());
        return false;
    }
}

/**
 * Get evaluation reports for a game
 * 
 * @param string $gameId  Game identifier
 * @param int    $limit   Number of reports to retrieve
 * @return array          Array of reports
 */
function SKAI_getEvaluationReports($gameId, $limit = 10) {
    try {
        $db = Factory::getDbo();
        $query = $db->getQuery(true);
        
        $query->select('*')
            ->from($db->quoteName('#__skai_evaluation_reports'))
            ->where($db->quoteName('game_id') . ' = ' . $db->quote($gameId))
            ->order($db->quoteName('report_date') . ' DESC')
            ->setLimit($limit);
        
        $db->setQuery($query);
        $results = $db->loadAssocList();
        
        // Decode JSON fields
        foreach ($results as &$row) {
            $row['top_miss_reasons_json'] = json_decode($row['top_miss_reasons_json'], true);
            if ($row['before_after_json']) {
                $row['before_after_json'] = json_decode($row['before_after_json'], true);
            }
        }
        
        return $results;
        
    } catch (\Exception $e) {
        error_log('[SKAI] getEvaluationReports error: ' . $e->getMessage());
        return [];
    }
}

/**
 * Generate before/after comparison when tuning is applied
 * 
 * @param string $gameId       Game identifier
 * @param array  $drawHistory  Historical draws
 * @param int    $compareStart Start index for comparison
 * @param int    $compareEnd   End index for comparison
 * @param array  $oldParams    Old parameters
 * @param array  $newParams    New (tuned) parameters
 * @param callable $predictFn  Prediction function
 * @return array               Before/after comparison
 */
function SKAI_generateBeforeAfterComparison($gameId, $drawHistory, $compareStart, $compareEnd, $oldParams, $newParams, $predictFn) {
    try {
        // Run backtest with old params
        $beforeResult = SKAI_runWalkForwardBacktest(
            $gameId,
            $drawHistory,
            $oldParams['window_size'] ?? 50,
            $compareStart,
            $compareEnd,
            $oldParams,
            $predictFn
        );
        
        // Run backtest with new params
        $afterResult = SKAI_runWalkForwardBacktest(
            $gameId,
            $drawHistory,
            $newParams['window_size'] ?? 50,
            $compareStart,
            $compareEnd,
            $newParams,
            $predictFn
        );
        
        return [
            'before' => $beforeResult['aggregate'],
            'after' => $afterResult['aggregate'],
            'improvement' => [
                'recall_at_20' => $afterResult['aggregate']['avg_recall_at_20'] - $beforeResult['aggregate']['avg_recall_at_20'],
                'ndcg_at_20' => $afterResult['aggregate']['avg_ndcg_at_20'] - $beforeResult['aggregate']['avg_ndcg_at_20'],
                'mean_winner_rank' => $beforeResult['aggregate']['avg_mean_winner_rank'] - $afterResult['aggregate']['avg_mean_winner_rank'], // Lower is better
                'top5_containment_rate' => (float)($afterResult['aggregate']['top5_containment_rate'] ?? 0.0) - (float)($beforeResult['aggregate']['top5_containment_rate'] ?? 0.0),
                'top10_containment_rate' => (float)($afterResult['aggregate']['top10_containment_rate'] ?? 0.0) - (float)($beforeResult['aggregate']['top10_containment_rate'] ?? 0.0),
                'mean_reciprocal_winner_rank' => (float)($afterResult['aggregate']['mean_reciprocal_winner_rank'] ?? 0.0) - (float)($beforeResult['aggregate']['mean_reciprocal_winner_rank'] ?? 0.0),
                'elite_objective_j' => (float)($afterResult['aggregate']['elite_objective_j'] ?? 0.0) - (float)($beforeResult['aggregate']['elite_objective_j'] ?? 0.0)
            ]
        ];
        
    } catch (\Exception $e) {
        error_log('[SKAI] generateBeforeAfterComparison error: ' . $e->getMessage());
        return [];
    }
}

// ========================================================
// [[SKAI2_SECURITY]]
// ========================================================

function SKAI2_sanitizeGameId($gameId)
{
    $s = preg_replace('/[^a-zA-Z0-9\-_.]/', '', (string)$gameId);
    return substr($s, 0, 50);
}

function SKAI2_sanitizeRunUuid($uuid)
{
    $s = preg_replace('/[^a-zA-Z0-9\-]/', '', (string)$uuid);
    return substr($s, 0, 64);
}

function SKAI2_safeJsonDecode($json, $maxSize = 1048576)
{
    if (!is_string($json) || strlen($json) > $maxSize) {
        return null;
    }
    $result = json_decode($json, true);
    return (json_last_error() === JSON_ERROR_NONE) ? $result : null;
}

function SKAI2_safeHtmlEncode($value)
{
    if (is_array($value) || is_object($value)) {
        $value = json_encode($value);
    }
    return htmlspecialchars((string)$value, ENT_QUOTES | ENT_HTML5, 'UTF-8');
}

function SKAI2_safeJsonEncode($data)
{
    $json = json_encode($data);
    return ($json === false) ? '{}' : $json;
}

function SKAI2_checkAdminAccess()
{
    try {
        $user = \Joomla\CMS\Factory::getApplication()->getIdentity();
        return $user && $user->authorise('core.admin');
    } catch (\Exception $e) {
        return false;
    }
}

// ========================================================
// [[SKAI2_GAME_RULES]]
// ========================================================

/**
 * Load raw lottery spec from the daily or master JSON feed.
 * Returns raw associative array on success, or empty array on failure.
 * Never throws - handles missing/unreadable/invalid JSON gracefully.
 */
function SKAI2_loadLotterySpecFromFeeds($gameId)
{
    $gameId = (string)$gameId;

    // --- Try daily lottery catalog first ---
    $dailyPaths = [];
    if (defined('JPATH_ROOT')) {
        $dailyPaths[] = JPATH_ROOT . '/dailylotteries.json';
    }
    $dailyPaths[] = __DIR__ . '/dailylotteries.json';

    foreach ($dailyPaths as $path) {
        if (!is_file($path) || !is_readable($path)) {
            continue;
        }
        $content = @file_get_contents($path);
        if ($content === false) {
            continue;
        }
        $decoded = SKAI2_safeJsonDecode($content);
        if (!is_array($decoded) || !isset($decoded['dailylotteries']) || !is_array($decoded['dailylotteries'])) {
            continue;
        }
        foreach ($decoded['dailylotteries'] as $entry) {
            if (is_array($entry) && isset($entry['gameId']) && (string)$entry['gameId'] === $gameId) {
                $entry['_source'] = 'daily';
                return $entry;
            }
        }
    }

    // --- Fall back to master skip-config catalog ---
    $masterPaths = [];
    if (defined('JPATH_ROOT')) {
        $masterPaths[] = JPATH_ROOT . '/lottery_skip_config.json';
    }
    $masterPaths[] = __DIR__ . '/lottery_skip_config.json';

    foreach ($masterPaths as $path) {
        if (!is_file($path) || !is_readable($path)) {
            continue;
        }
        $content = @file_get_contents($path);
        if ($content === false) {
            continue;
        }
        $decoded = SKAI2_safeJsonDecode($content);
        if (!is_array($decoded) || !isset($decoded['lotteries']) || !is_array($decoded['lotteries'])) {
            continue;
        }
        // keyed by gameId
        if (isset($decoded['lotteries'][$gameId]) && is_array($decoded['lotteries'][$gameId])) {
            $entry = $decoded['lotteries'][$gameId];
            $entry['_source'] = 'master';
            return $entry;
        }
        // linear search
        foreach ($decoded['lotteries'] as $entry) {
            if (is_array($entry) && isset($entry['gameId']) && (string)$entry['gameId'] === $gameId) {
                $entry['_source'] = 'master';
                return $entry;
            }
        }
    }

    return [];
}

/**
 * Normalize a raw spec from either feed format into the canonical SKAI2 game rules object.
 */
function SKAI2_normalizeLotterySpec($rawSpec, $gameId)
{
    $canonical = [
        'game_id'         => (string)$gameId,
        'game_name'       => '',
        'game_type'       => 'regular',
        'main_pool_min'   => 1,
        'main_pool_max'   => 0,
        'main_pick'       => 0,
        'has_bonus'       => false,
        'bonus_pool_min'  => 1,
        'bonus_pool_max'  => 0,
        'bonus_pick'      => 0,
        'ordered'         => false,
        'is_daily_digit'  => false,
        'digit_positions' => 0,
        'digit_min'       => 0,
        'digit_max'       => 9,
        'source'          => '',
        'source_valid'    => false,
        'warnings'        => [],
    ];

    if (empty($rawSpec) || !is_array($rawSpec)) {
        $canonical['warnings'][] = 'Empty or invalid raw spec for game_id=' . $gameId;
        return $canonical;
    }

    $source = isset($rawSpec['_source']) ? (string)$rawSpec['_source'] : '';
    if ($source === '') {
        if (isset($rawSpec['pickSize']) || isset($rawSpec['rangeMain'])) {
            $source = 'daily';
        } elseif (isset($rawSpec['lotteryConfig'])) {
            $source = 'master';
        }
    }
    $canonical['source'] = $source;

    if ($source === 'daily') {
        $label          = isset($rawSpec['label'])          ? (string)$rawSpec['label']      : $gameId;
        $pickSize       = isset($rawSpec['pickSize'])        ? (int)$rawSpec['pickSize']      : 0;
        $rangeMain      = isset($rawSpec['rangeMain'])       ? (int)$rawSpec['rangeMain']     : 0;
        $extraBallCount = isset($rawSpec['extraBallCount'])  ? (int)$rawSpec['extraBallCount']: 0;
        $rangeExtra     = isset($rawSpec['rangeExtra'])      ? (int)$rawSpec['rangeExtra']    : 0;

        $canonical['game_name'] = $label;

        if ($rangeMain > 0 && $rangeMain <= 10) {
            // digit-based daily lottery (Pick 2 / Pick 3 / Pick 4 / Pick 5)
            $canonical['is_daily_digit']  = true;
            $canonical['game_type']       = 'daily_digit';
            $canonical['ordered']         = true;
            $canonical['digit_positions'] = $pickSize;
            $canonical['digit_min']       = 0;
            $canonical['digit_max']       = $rangeMain - 1;
            $canonical['main_pool_min']   = 0;
            $canonical['main_pool_max']   = $rangeMain - 1;
            $canonical['main_pick']       = $pickSize;
        } else {
            $canonical['game_type']     = 'regular';
            $canonical['main_pool_min'] = 1;
            $canonical['main_pool_max'] = $rangeMain;
            $canonical['main_pick']     = $pickSize;
        }

        if ($extraBallCount > 0 && $rangeExtra > 0) {
            $canonical['has_bonus']      = true;
            $canonical['bonus_pool_min'] = 1;
            $canonical['bonus_pool_max'] = $rangeExtra;
            $canonical['bonus_pick']     = $extraBallCount;
            if ($canonical['game_type'] === 'regular') {
                $canonical['game_type'] = 'bonus';
            }
        }

        $canonical['source_valid'] = ($pickSize > 0 && $rangeMain > 0);

    } elseif ($source === 'master') {
        $name = isset($rawSpec['lotteryName']) ? (string)$rawSpec['lotteryName'] : $gameId;
        $canonical['game_name'] = $name;

        $lc = (isset($rawSpec['lotteryConfig']) && is_array($rawSpec['lotteryConfig']))
            ? $rawSpec['lotteryConfig'] : [];

        $numMain  = isset($lc['num_main_balls_drawn'])  ? (int)$lc['num_main_balls_drawn']  : 0;
        $maxMain  = isset($lc['max_main_ball_number'])  ? (int)$lc['max_main_ball_number']  : 0;
        $hasExtra = isset($lc['has_extra_ball'])        ? (bool)$lc['has_extra_ball']        : false;
        $numExtra = isset($lc['num_extra_balls_drawn']) ? (int)$lc['num_extra_balls_drawn'] : 0;
        $maxExtra = isset($lc['max_extra_ball_number']) ? (int)$lc['max_extra_ball_number'] : 0;

        $canonical['main_pool_min'] = 1;
        $canonical['main_pool_max'] = $maxMain;
        $canonical['main_pick']     = $numMain;

        if ($hasExtra && $maxExtra > 0) {
            $canonical['has_bonus']      = true;
            $canonical['bonus_pool_min'] = 1;
            $canonical['bonus_pool_max'] = $maxExtra;
            $canonical['bonus_pick']     = max(1, $numExtra);
            $canonical['game_type']      = 'bonus';
        }

        $canonical['source_valid'] = ($numMain > 0 && $maxMain > 0);

    } else {
        $canonical['warnings'][] = 'Unknown spec format; could not normalize game_id=' . $gameId;
    }

    return $canonical;
}

function SKAI2_validateLotterySpec($rules)
{
    $errors = [];
    if (empty($rules['game_id'])) {
        $errors[] = 'Missing game_id';
    }
    if ((int)$rules['main_pool_max'] <= 0) {
        $errors[] = 'main_pool_max must be positive';
    }
    if ((int)$rules['main_pick'] <= 0) {
        $errors[] = 'main_pick must be positive';
    }
    $poolSize = (int)$rules['main_pool_max'] - (int)$rules['main_pool_min'] + 1;
    if ($poolSize > 0 && (int)$rules['main_pick'] > $poolSize) {
        $errors[] = 'main_pick exceeds pool size';
    }
    if (!empty($rules['has_bonus'])) {
        if ((int)$rules['bonus_pool_max'] <= 0) {
            $errors[] = 'bonus_pool_max must be positive when has_bonus is true';
        }
        if ((int)$rules['bonus_pick'] <= 0) {
            $errors[] = 'bonus_pick must be positive when has_bonus is true';
        }
    }
    return ['valid' => empty($errors), 'errors' => $errors];
}

/**
 * Main entry point - load, normalize, validate, and return canonical game rules.
 * Uses static cache per gameId.
 */
function SKAI2_getGameRules($gameId)
{
    static $cache = [];
    $gameId = SKAI2_sanitizeGameId((string)$gameId);
    if (isset($cache[$gameId])) {
        return $cache[$gameId];
    }
    $rawSpec    = SKAI2_loadLotterySpecFromFeeds($gameId);
    $rules      = SKAI2_normalizeLotterySpec($rawSpec, $gameId);
    $validation = SKAI2_validateLotterySpec($rules);
    if (!$validation['valid']) {
        $rules['warnings'] = array_merge($rules['warnings'], $validation['errors']);
    }
    $cache[$gameId] = $rules;
    return $rules;
}

function SKAI2_normalizeGameRules($rawSpec, $gameId = '')
{
    return SKAI2_normalizeLotterySpec($rawSpec, $gameId);
}

function SKAI2_detectGameType($rules)
{
    if (!empty($rules['is_daily_digit'])) {
        return 'daily_digit';
    }
    if (!empty($rules['has_bonus'])) {
        return 'bonus';
    }
    return 'regular';
}

function SKAI2_buildNumberDomain($rules)
{
    if (!empty($rules['is_daily_digit'])) {
        $dmin = isset($rules['digit_min']) ? (int)$rules['digit_min'] : 0;
        $dmax = isset($rules['digit_max']) ? (int)$rules['digit_max'] : 9;
        return range($dmin, $dmax);
    }
    $min = isset($rules['main_pool_min']) ? (int)$rules['main_pool_min'] : 1;
    $max = isset($rules['main_pool_max']) ? (int)$rules['main_pool_max'] : 0;
    return ($max >= $min) ? range($min, $max) : [];
}

function SKAI2_buildBonusDomain($rules)
{
    if (empty($rules['has_bonus'])) {
        return [];
    }
    $min = isset($rules['bonus_pool_min']) ? (int)$rules['bonus_pool_min'] : 1;
    $max = isset($rules['bonus_pool_max']) ? (int)$rules['bonus_pool_max'] : 0;
    return ($max >= $min) ? range($min, $max) : [];
}

function SKAI2_isDailyDigitGame($rules)
{
    return !empty($rules['is_daily_digit']);
}

function SKAI2_isRegularCombinationGame($rules)
{
    return empty($rules['is_daily_digit']);
}

function SKAI2_validateGameRules($rules)
{
    if (empty($rules) || !is_array($rules)) {
        return ['valid' => false, 'errors' => ['Game rules missing or invalid']];
    }
    $validation = SKAI2_validateLotterySpec($rules);
    if (!is_array($validation)) {
        return ['valid' => false, 'errors' => ['Game rule validation failed']];
    }
    return [
        'valid'  => !empty($validation['valid']),
        'errors' => (isset($validation['errors']) && is_array($validation['errors'])) ? $validation['errors'] : [],
    ];
}

// ========================================================
// [[SKAI2_CORE_MATH]]
// ========================================================

/**
 * Exact integer combination count C(n, k).
 * Uses the multiplicative formula to avoid overflow for typical lottery sizes (n <= 100).
 * Returns 0.0 for invalid inputs (n < 0, k < 0, k > n).
 */
function SKAI2_combinationCount($n, $k)
{
    $n = (int)$n;
    $k = (int)$k;
    if ($n < 0 || $k < 0 || $k > $n) {
        return 0.0;
    }
    if ($k === 0 || $k === $n) {
        return 1.0;
    }
    // Use the smaller of k and n-k to minimize iterations
    if ($k > $n - $k) {
        $k = $n - $k;
    }
    $result = 1.0;
    for ($i = 0; $i < $k; $i++) {
        $result *= (float)($n - $i);
        $result /= (float)($i + 1);
    }
    return $result;
}

/**
 * Exact N-th harmonic number H_N = 1 + 1/2 + 1/3 + ... + 1/N.
 * Used for the exact expected MRR of a random ranker.
 * Returns 0.0 for N <= 0.
 */
function SKAI2_harmonicNumber($n)
{
    $n = (int)$n;
    if ($n <= 0) {
        return 0.0;
    }
    $h = 0.0;
    for ($i = 1; $i <= $n; $i++) {
        $h += 1.0 / (float)$i;
    }
    return $h;
}

function SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($rules)
{
    if (!empty($rules['is_daily_digit'])) {
        $range = (int)$rules['digit_max'] - (int)$rules['digit_min'] + 1;
        return ($range > 0) ? 1.0 / (float)$range : 0.0;
    }
    $poolSize = (int)$rules['main_pool_max'] - (int)$rules['main_pool_min'] + 1;
    if ($poolSize <= 0) {
        return 0.0;
    }
    return (float)$rules['main_pick'] / (float)$poolSize;
}

function SKAI2_calculateExpectedRandomMatches($rules)
{
    $poolSize = (int)$rules['main_pool_max'] - (int)$rules['main_pool_min'] + 1;
    if ($poolSize <= 0) {
        return 0.0;
    }
    $pick = (float)$rules['main_pick'];
    return ($pick * $pick) / (float)$poolSize;
}

/**
 * Exact probability of at least one match between a random K-pick and M winning numbers
 * in a pool of N using exact hypergeometric math.
 *
 * P(at least 1 match) = 1 - C(N-M, K) / C(N, K)
 *
 * Where:
 *   N = main pool size
 *   M = number of winning main numbers (main_pick)
 *   K = size of the predicted set (same as main_pick for default comparison)
 *
 * The previous formula 1 - (1 - p)^K used a binomial independence approximation
 * that is inaccurate for sampling without replacement (which is how lotteries work).
 * The hypergeometric formula is exact.
 */
function SKAI2_calculateHitAtLeastOneProbability($rules)
{
    $poolSize = (int)$rules['main_pool_max'] - (int)$rules['main_pool_min'] + 1;
    $pick     = (int)$rules['main_pick'];
    if ($poolSize <= 0 || $pick <= 0 || $pick > $poolSize) {
        return 0.0;
    }
    // N = poolSize, M = pick (winning set size), K = pick (predicted set size)
    $cNK    = SKAI2_combinationCount($poolSize, $pick);
    if ($cNK <= 0.0) {
        return 0.0;
    }
    $cNoHit = SKAI2_combinationCount($poolSize - $pick, $pick);
    return 1.0 - ($cNoHit / $cNK);
}

/**
 * Expected random Recall@K for this game.
 *
 * Derivation (hypergeometric mean):
 *   If we randomly select K numbers from a pool of N and the winning set has M
 *   correct numbers, the expected overlap is K * M / N.
 *   Recall@K = E[overlap] / M = K / N.
 *
 * The previous formula min(K, pick) * pick/poolSize was incorrect because:
 *   - It saturates at pick * pick/poolSize regardless of K > pick, so
 *     recall@10 and recall@20 returned the same value.
 *   - For a 6/49 game with K=20 it returned min(20,6) * 6/49 = 6*6/49 ~= 0.735
 *     instead of the correct 20/49 ~= 0.408.
 * This affected every random-vs-engine comparison and all learning promotion gates.
 */
function SKAI2_calculateRandomRecallAtK($rules, $k)
{
    $poolSize = (int)$rules['main_pool_max'] - (int)$rules['main_pool_min'] + 1;
    if ($poolSize <= 0) {
        return 0.0;
    }
    // Recall@K = K/poolSize, capped at 1.0 when K >= poolSize.
    return min(1.0, (float)min((int)$k, $poolSize) / (float)$poolSize);
}

/**
 * Expected MRR for a random ranker on this game.
 *
 * Definition: For a random ranking of N numbers where M are relevant (winning),
 * the expected Mean Reciprocal Rank is:
 *
 *   E[MRR] = H_N / N
 *
 * Derivation: For a single relevant item randomly placed in a list of N,
 * E[1/rank] = (1/N) * sum_{k=1}^{N} 1/k = H_N / N.
 * By linearity of expectation across M winners: E[MRR] = H_N / N (does not depend on M).
 *
 * The previous formula pick / (poolSize + 1) was incorrect because:
 * - It scales with pick (number of winners), which is not how MRR is defined.
 * - For 6/49: 6/50 = 0.12, vs correct H_49/49 = 4.499/49 ~= 0.0918.
 */
function SKAI2_calculateExpectedRandomMRR($rules)
{
    $poolSize = (int)$rules['main_pool_max'] - (int)$rules['main_pool_min'] + 1;
    if ($poolSize <= 0) {
        return 0.0;
    }
    return SKAI2_harmonicNumber($poolSize) / (float)$poolSize;
}

function SKAI2_calculateExpectedRandomNDCG($rules, $k = 20)
{
    $poolSize = (int)$rules['main_pool_max'] - (int)$rules['main_pool_min'] + 1;
    if ($poolSize <= 0) {
        return 0.0;
    }
    $pick = (int)$rules['main_pick'];
    $pHit = (float)$pick / (float)$poolSize;
    $dcg  = 0.0;
    for ($i = 0; $i < $k; $i++) {
        $dcg += $pHit / log($i + 2, 2);
    }
    $idcg = 0.0;
    for ($i = 0; $i < $pick && $i < $k; $i++) {
        $idcg += 1.0 / log($i + 2, 2);
    }
    return ($idcg > 0.0) ? $dcg / $idcg : 0.0;
}

function SKAI2_buildRandomBaselineForGame($rules)
{
    $poolSize = (int)$rules['main_pool_max'] - (int)$rules['main_pool_min'] + 1;
    // Flat keys kept for backward compatibility with all existing callers.
    $flat = [
        'base_probability'             => SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($rules),
        'expected_random_matches'      => SKAI2_calculateExpectedRandomMatches($rules),
        'hit_at_least_one_probability' => SKAI2_calculateHitAtLeastOneProbability($rules),
        'recall_at_5'                  => SKAI2_calculateRandomRecallAtK($rules, 5),
        'recall_at_10'                 => SKAI2_calculateRandomRecallAtK($rules, 10),
        'recall_at_20'                 => SKAI2_calculateRandomRecallAtK($rules, 20),
        'expected_mrr'                 => SKAI2_calculateExpectedRandomMRR($rules),
        'expected_ndcg_20'             => SKAI2_calculateExpectedRandomNDCG($rules, 20),
        'pool_size'                    => $poolSize,
        'pick_size'                    => (int)$rules['main_pick'],
    ];
    // Structured keys required by SKAI2_runPredictionEngine and downstream callers.
    $flat['main']        = $flat;
    // Bonus baseline: populated for bonus games with correct hypergeometric formula.
    if (!empty($rules['has_bonus']) && !empty($rules['bonus_pool_size'])) {
        $bonusBaseline = SKAI2_buildBonusRandomBaseline($rules);
        $flat['bonus'] = $bonusBaseline;
        // Flat backward-compatible bonus keys for callers that read top-level keys.
        $flat['bonus_base_probability'] = $bonusBaseline['bonus_base_probability'] ?? $bonusBaseline['base_probability'];
        $flat['bonus_hit_at_least_one'] = $bonusBaseline['hit_at_least_one'];
        $flat['bonus_pool_size']        = $bonusBaseline['bonus_pool_size'];
        $flat['bonus_pick']             = $bonusBaseline['bonus_pick'];
    } else {
        $flat['bonus'] = [];
    }
    $flat['daily_digit'] = [];
    $flat['game_type']   = SKAI2_detectGameType($rules);
    return $flat;
}

// ========================================================
// [[SKAI2_SCORING_ENGINE]]
// ========================================================

/**
 * Return shared sample-size thresholds for confidence/evidence grading.
 * Centralizing avoids duplication between calculateFinalNumberScores and calculateEvidenceGrade.
 * Boundaries: E <= 9 < D <= 29 < C <= 99 < B <= 299 < A
 */
function SKAI2_getSampleSizeGradeThresholds()
{
    return ['E_max' => 9, 'D_max' => 29, 'C_max' => 99, 'B_max' => 299];
}

function SKAI2_getDefaultSignalCaps()
{
    return [
        'frequency' => 0.20,
        'skip'      => 0.15,
        'recency'   => 0.15,
        'stability' => 0.15,
        'pairwise'  => 0.10,
    ];
}

function SKAI2_applyConfidencePenalty($score, $confidence)
{
    return (float)$score * max(0.0, min(1.0, (float)$confidence));
}

/**
 * Shrink a signal toward zero when sample size is below the required minimum.
 * Factor = sampleSize / minRequired, clamped to [0, 1].
 */
function SKAI2_applyLowSamplePenalty($score, $sampleSize, $minRequired = 30)
{
    if ($sampleSize >= $minRequired) {
        return (float)$score;
    }
    $factor = max(0.0, min(1.0, (float)$sampleSize / (float)max(1, $minRequired)));
    return (float)$score * $factor;
}

// Minimum sample size below which overfitting risk is evaluated (approx. reliable frequency floor).
if (!defined('SKAI2_OVERFIT_MIN_SAMPLE'))       { define('SKAI2_OVERFIT_MIN_SAMPLE', 25); }
// Signal-strength floor: scores at or below this value are already weak; no extra penalty needed.
if (!defined('SKAI2_OVERFIT_SIGNAL_BASELINE'))  { define('SKAI2_OVERFIT_SIGNAL_BASELINE', 0.3); }
// Maximum cluster proximity (gap <= this = clustered pair).
if (!defined('SKAI2_CLUSTER_PROXIMITY_THRESHOLD')) { define('SKAI2_CLUSTER_PROXIMITY_THRESHOLD', 2); }
// Skip signal cap: maximum absolute skip adjustment (matches getDefaultSignalCaps 'skip').
if (!defined('SKAI2_MAX_SKIP_CAP'))             { define('SKAI2_MAX_SKIP_CAP', 0.15); }
// Learning cap bounds: signal caps may not be tuned outside this range.
if (!defined('SKAI2_LEARNING_MAX_CAP'))         { define('SKAI2_LEARNING_MAX_CAP', 0.30); }
if (!defined('SKAI2_LEARNING_MIN_CAP'))         { define('SKAI2_LEARNING_MIN_CAP', 0.05); }
// Minimum draws required before learning promotion gates are evaluated.
if (!defined('SKAI2_MIN_EVIDENCE_DRAWS'))       { define('SKAI2_MIN_EVIDENCE_DRAWS', 30); }
// Minimum improvement over random baseline required for Gate 7 to pass.
// 0.001 = 0.1 percentage point above random recall@20 as a floor.
if (!defined('SKAI2_MIN_LEARNING_IMPROVEMENT')) { define('SKAI2_MIN_LEARNING_IMPROVEMENT', 0.001); }

/**
 * Penalize high-signal scores when sample is small to reduce overfitting risk.
 * Penalty engages only below SKAI2_OVERFIT_MIN_SAMPLE draws AND when signal strength
 * exceeds SKAI2_OVERFIT_SIGNAL_BASELINE.
 * The threshold of 25 is the production-safe minimum for reliable frequency estimates;
 * 0.3 is the signal-strength floor below which the score is already weak enough
 * to not warrant additional penalization.
 */
function SKAI2_applyOverfitPenalty($score, $sampleSize, $signalStrength)
{
    $overfitMinSample = defined('SKAI2_OVERFIT_MIN_SAMPLE')
        ? (int) SKAI2_OVERFIT_MIN_SAMPLE
        : 25;
    if (!defined('SKAI2_OVERFIT_MIN_SAMPLE')) {
        static $skai2OverfitMinFallbackLogged = false;
        if (!$skai2OverfitMinFallbackLogged) {
            $skai2DebugEnabled = false;
            if (isset($GLOBALS['isDebug'])) {
                $skai2DebugEnabled = (bool)$GLOBALS['isDebug'];
            } else {
                try {
                    $skai2DebugEnabled = (bool)\Joomla\CMS\Factory::getConfig()->get('debug');
                } catch (\Throwable $_skai2DbgEx) {
                    $skai2DebugEnabled = false;
                }
            }
            if ($skai2DebugEnabled) {
                error_log('[SKAI2] SKAI2_OVERFIT_MIN_SAMPLE undefined; fallback default 25 applied.');
            }
            $skai2OverfitMinFallbackLogged = true;
        }
    }
    if ($overfitMinSample < 5) {
        $overfitMinSample = 5;
    }
    if ($sampleSize >= $overfitMinSample || $signalStrength <= SKAI2_OVERFIT_SIGNAL_BASELINE) {
        return (float)$score;
    }
    $riskFactor    = (1.0 - (float)$sampleSize / (float)$overfitMinSample)
                   * (($signalStrength - SKAI2_OVERFIT_SIGNAL_BASELINE) / max(0.001, $signalStrength));
    $penaltyFactor = max(0.5, 1.0 - min(0.5, $riskFactor));
    return (float)$score * $penaltyFactor;
}

function SKAI2_applySignalCaps($scoreParts, $caps = [])
{
    $defaults = SKAI2_getDefaultSignalCaps();
    foreach ($defaults as $k => $v) {
        if (!isset($caps[$k])) {
            $caps[$k] = $v;
        }
    }
    $capped = $scoreParts;
    foreach (['frequency', 'skip', 'recency', 'stability', 'pairwise'] as $component) {
        if (isset($capped[$component], $caps[$component])) {
            $cap              = (float)$caps[$component];
            $capped[$component] = max(-$cap, min($cap, (float)$capped[$component]));
        }
    }
    return $capped;
}

/**
 * Penalize signals that vary wildly across time windows (sign of overfitting or noise).
 * Threshold: stdDev > 50% of mean -> apply 20% penalty.
 */
function SKAI2_applyInstabilityPenalty($score, $windowRates)
{
    if (empty($windowRates)) {
        return (float)$score;
    }
    $values = array_values($windowRates);
    $n      = count($values);
    $mean   = array_sum($values) / $n;
    $var    = 0.0;
    foreach ($values as $r) {
        $var += ($r - $mean) * ($r - $mean);
    }
    $stdDev = sqrt($var / $n);
    if ($stdDev > 0.5 * max(0.0001, $mean)) {
        return (float)$score * 0.8;
    }
    return (float)$score;
}

function SKAI2_normalizeComponentScores($scores)
{
    if (empty($scores)) {
        return [];
    }
    $values = array_values($scores);
    $min    = min($values);
    $max    = max($values);
    if (($max - $min) < 1.0e-9) {
        return array_fill_keys(array_keys($scores), 0.0);
    }
    $result = [];
    foreach ($scores as $k => $v) {
        $result[$k] = 2.0 * (((float)$v - $min) / ($max - $min)) - 1.0;
    }
    return $result;
}

function SKAI2_calculateSignalConfidence($sampleSize, $signalStrength, $windowStability)
{
    $c = min(1.0, sqrt((float)$sampleSize / 100.0)) * max(0.0, min(1.0, (float)$windowStability));
    return max(0.0, min(1.0, $c));
}

function SKAI2_calculateCurrentSkip($num, $drawHistory)
{
    $n = count($drawHistory);
    for ($i = $n - 1; $i >= 0; $i--) {
        $draw = $drawHistory[$i];
        if (isset($draw['main']) && is_array($draw['main']) && in_array($num, $draw['main'], true)) {
            return $n - 1 - $i;
        }
    }
    return $n;
}

function SKAI2_bucketSkipValue($skip)
{
    $s = (int)$skip;
    if ($s <= 2)  { return 'fresh'; }
    if ($s <= 7)  { return 'short'; }
    if ($s <= 15) { return 'medium'; }
    if ($s <= 30) { return 'long'; }
    return 'very_long';
}

/**
 * Bayesian/Laplace smoothed frequency.
 * Adjusts observed hit rate toward the expected random rate.
 * alpha is the Laplace smoothing parameter (1.0 = add-one smoothing).
 */
function SKAI2_calculateSmoothedFrequency($num, $drawHistory, $gameRules, $alpha = 1.0)
{
    $totalDraws = count($drawHistory);
    $domain     = SKAI2_buildNumberDomain($gameRules);
    $poolSize   = max(1, count($domain));

    $rawCount = 0;
    foreach ($drawHistory as $draw) {
        if (isset($draw['main']) && is_array($draw['main']) && in_array($num, $draw['main'], true)) {
            $rawCount++;
        }
    }

    $expectedRate  = SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($gameRules);
    $observedRate  = ($totalDraws > 0) ? (float)$rawCount / (float)$totalDraws : 0.0;
    // Correct Bayesian/Laplace smoothing using the game-specific base probability as the prior mean.
    // Prior mean = expectedRate = main_pick / poolSize.
    // The previous formula used alpha / (totalDraws + alpha * poolSize) as the prior, which
    // effectively pulled toward 1/poolSize (uniform) instead of pick/poolSize (correct).
    // This formula: smoothedRate = (rawCount + alpha * expectedRate) / (totalDraws + alpha)
    // - When totalDraws is large: smoothedRate -> observedRate (data dominates)
    // - When totalDraws is small: smoothedRate -> expectedRate (prior dominates)
    $smoothedRate  = ((float)$rawCount + (float)$alpha * $expectedRate)
                   / max(1.0, (float)$totalDraws + (float)$alpha);

    $adjustment = ($expectedRate > 0.0) ? ($smoothedRate - $expectedRate) / $expectedRate : 0.0;
    $adjustment = max(-1.0, min(1.0, $adjustment));

    $confidence = ($totalDraws > 0)
        ? min(1.0, sqrt((float)$totalDraws / 100.0))
        : 0.0;

    return [
        'raw_count'     => $rawCount,
        'observed_rate' => $observedRate,
        'expected_rate' => $expectedRate,
        'smoothed_rate' => $smoothedRate,
        'adjustment'    => $adjustment,
        'confidence'    => $confidence,
    ];
}

// ========================================================
// [[SKAI2_SKIP_SUB_FUNCTIONS]]
// Section 7: Named sub-functions extracted from conditional skip.
// These are used by SKAI2_calculateConditionalSkip and may be called directly.
// ========================================================

/**
 * Return raw hit-rate data for a number when its skip matched a given bucket.
 * Exposed as a named function for testing and module reuse.
 */
function SKAI2_calculateConditionalSkipHitRate($num, $skipBucket, $drawHistory, $gameRules)
{
    $n        = count($drawHistory);
    $baseRate = SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($gameRules);

    $cases       = 0;
    $hits        = 0;
    $isHit0      = isset($drawHistory[0]['main'])
                && is_array($drawHistory[0]['main'])
                && in_array($num, $drawHistory[0]['main'], true);
    $lastSeenIdx = $isHit0 ? 0 : -1;

    for ($i = 1; $i < $n; $i++) {
        $skipBefore = ($lastSeenIdx < 0) ? $i : ($i - 1 - $lastSeenIdx);
        $draw       = $drawHistory[$i];
        $isHit      = isset($draw['main']) && is_array($draw['main'])
                   && in_array($num, $draw['main'], true);
        if (SKAI2_bucketSkipValue($skipBefore) === $skipBucket) {
            $cases++;
            if ($isHit) {
                $hits++;
            }
        }
        if ($isHit) {
            $lastSeenIdx = $i;
        }
    }

    $hitRate      = ($cases > 0) ? (float)$hits / (float)$cases : $baseRate;
    $alpha        = 1.0;
    // Correct Bayesian smoothing: numerator uses $hits (actual successes), not $cases.
    // Using $cases here would assume every case was a hit, pulling smoothed rate toward 1.
    $smoothedRate = ((float)$hits + $alpha * $baseRate) / ((float)$cases + $alpha);

    return [
        'skip_bucket'      => $skipBucket,
        'cases'            => $cases,
        'hits'             => $hits,
        'rate'             => $hitRate,
        'smoothed_rate'    => $smoothedRate,
        'expected_rate'    => $baseRate,
    ];
}

/**
 * Calculate skip signal adjustment from smoothed hit rate vs. base rate.
 * Returns a value capped to SKAI2_MAX_SKIP_CAP.
 */
function SKAI2_calculateSkipAdjustment($smoothedHitRate, $baseRate)
{
    $rawAdj = ($baseRate > 0.0) ? ($smoothedHitRate - $baseRate) / $baseRate : 0.0;
    return max(-SKAI2_MAX_SKIP_CAP, min(SKAI2_MAX_SKIP_CAP, $rawAdj));
}

/**
 * Calculate skip signal confidence from number of cases observed.
 */
function SKAI2_calculateSkipConfidence($cases)
{
    return min(1.0, sqrt((float)max(0, $cases) / 30.0));
}

// ========================================================
// [[SKAI2_RECENCY_SUB_FUNCTIONS]]
// Section 8: Named sub-functions for recency/stability.
// ========================================================

/**
 * Score a single recency window for a given number.
 * Returns raw rate, hit count, window size, and base rate for comparison.
 */
function SKAI2_calculateRecencyWindowScore($num, $drawHistory, $windowSize, $gameRules)
{
    $n        = count($drawHistory);
    $baseRate = SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($gameRules);
    $slice    = ($n >= $windowSize)
              ? array_slice($drawHistory, $n - $windowSize)
              : $drawHistory;
    $cnt  = count($slice);
    $hits = 0;
    foreach ($slice as $draw) {
        if (isset($draw['main']) && is_array($draw['main'])
            && in_array($num, $draw['main'], true)
        ) {
            $hits++;
        }
    }
    $rate = ($cnt > 0) ? (float)$hits / (float)$cnt : $baseRate;
    return [
        'window_size' => $windowSize,
        'hits'        => $hits,
        'count'       => $cnt,
        'rate'        => $rate,
        'base_rate'   => $baseRate,
    ];
}

/**
 * Calculate stability across a set of window rates.
 * Returns 1.0 (perfectly stable) down to 0.0 (highly variable).
 */
function SKAI2_calculateMultiWindowStability($windowRates)
{
    $vals = array_values((array)$windowRates);
    $n    = count($vals);
    if ($n === 0) {
        return 0.5;
    }
    $mean = array_sum($vals) / $n;
    $var  = 0.0;
    foreach ($vals as $r) {
        $var += ($r - $mean) * ($r - $mean);
    }
    $stdDev = sqrt($var / $n);
    return 1.0 - min(1.0, $stdDev / max(0.0001, $mean));
}

/**
 * Compare recent window rate to long-term rate and base rate.
 * Returns lift values and trending flags.
 */
function SKAI2_compareRecentToLongTerm($recentRate, $longTermRate, $baseRate)
{
    $recentLift   = ($baseRate > 0.0) ? ($recentRate   - $baseRate) / $baseRate : 0.0;
    $ltLift       = ($baseRate > 0.0) ? ($longTermRate  - $baseRate) / $baseRate : 0.0;
    $delta        = $recentLift - $ltLift;
    return [
        'recent_lift'    => $recentLift,
        'long_term_lift' => $ltLift,
        'delta'          => $delta,
        'trending_up'    => ($delta > 0.05),
        'trending_down'  => ($delta < -0.05),
    ];
}

/**
 * Calculate stability adjustment from long-term lift and stability score.
 * Capped at +/- 15%.
 */
function SKAI2_calculateStabilityAdjustment($stability, $longTermLift)
{
    $base = (float)$longTermLift * 0.1 * (float)$stability;
    return max(-0.15, min(0.15, $base));
}

// ========================================================
// [[SKAI2_PAIRWISE_SUB_FUNCTIONS]]
// Section 9: Named sub-functions for pairwise analysis.
// ========================================================

/**
 * Calculate raw co-occurrence statistics for a pair of numbers.
 * Returns structured stats used by lift and shrinkage functions.
 */
function SKAI2_calculatePairwiseStats($numI, $numJ, $drawHistory)
{
    $together   = 0;
    $iHits      = 0;
    $jHits      = 0;
    $totalDraws = count($drawHistory);
    foreach ($drawHistory as $draw) {
        if (!isset($draw['main']) || !is_array($draw['main'])) {
            continue;
        }
        $hasI = in_array($numI, $draw['main'], true);
        $hasJ = in_array($numJ, $draw['main'], true);
        if ($hasI) { $iHits++; }
        if ($hasJ) { $jHits++; }
        if ($hasI && $hasJ) { $together++; }
    }
    return [
        'num_i'        => $numI,
        'num_j'        => $numJ,
        'i_hits'       => $iHits,
        'j_hits'       => $jHits,
        'together'     => $together,
        'total_draws'  => $totalDraws,
    ];
}

/**
 * Calculate raw pairwise lift: observed co-occurrence vs. expected by independence.
 * lift = (observed pairs) / (base_rate * i_hits).
 * Returns 1.0 (neutral) when sample is too small.
 */
function SKAI2_calculatePairwiseLift($stats, $baseRate)
{
    if ($stats['i_hits'] < 1 || $baseRate <= 0.0) {
        return 1.0;
    }
    $expected = (float)$baseRate * (float)$stats['i_hits'];
    return ($expected > 0.0) ? (float)$stats['together'] / $expected : 1.0;
}

/**
 * Shrink raw pairwise lift toward 1.0 (neutral) based on sample size.
 * Prevents overreaction to rare coincidental pairs.
 * alpha controls how many pseudo-observations pull toward neutral.
 */
function SKAI2_applyPairwiseShrinkage($rawLift, $sampleSize, $alpha = 1.0)
{
    $n      = max(0.0, (float)$sampleSize);
    $weight = $n / ($n + (float)$alpha);
    return 1.0 + ($rawLift - 1.0) * $weight;
}

/**
 * Calculate pairwise confidence from sample size.
 * 50 observations is considered full confidence.
 */
function SKAI2_calculatePairwiseConfidence($sampleSize)
{
    return min(1.0, sqrt((float)max(0, $sampleSize) / 50.0));
}

/**
 * Calculate a set-level pairwise compatibility score for a full combination.
 * Returns [0, 1] where > 0.5 means above-average pairwise lift.
 * Returns 0.5 when insufficient data.
 *
 * Structured pair output for any pair is available via SKAI2_calculatePairwiseStats().
 */
function SKAI2_calculateSetCompatibilityScore($combo, $drawHistory, $gameRules)
{
    $combo = array_values((array)$combo);
    if (count($combo) < 2 || empty($drawHistory)) {
        return 0.5;
    }
    $baseRate   = SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($gameRules);
    $totalLift  = 0.0;
    $pairs      = 0;
    $cnt        = count($combo);
    for ($i = 0; $i < $cnt; $i++) {
        for ($j = $i + 1; $j < $cnt; $j++) {
            $stats = SKAI2_calculatePairwiseStats($combo[$i], $combo[$j], $drawHistory);
            if ($stats['i_hits'] >= 3) {
                $rawLift   = SKAI2_calculatePairwiseLift($stats, $baseRate);
                $shrunk    = SKAI2_applyPairwiseShrinkage($rawLift, $stats['i_hits']);
                $totalLift += $shrunk;
                $pairs++;
            }
        }
    }
    if ($pairs === 0) {
        return 0.5;
    }
    $avgLift = $totalLift / $pairs;
    // Map avgLift to [0, 1]: neutral at 1.0, capped at 2.0
    return max(0.0, min(1.0, ($avgLift - 0.5) / max(0.001, $avgLift)));
}

// ========================================================
// [[SKAI2_COMBINATION_SUB_FUNCTIONS]]
// Section 13: Additional combination scoring functions.
// ========================================================

/**
 * Score combination diversity (spread + cluster control combined).
 */
function SKAI2_scoreCombinationDiversity($combo, $rules)
{
    return SKAI2_scoreSpread($combo, $rules) * 0.5
         + SKAI2_scoreClusterControl($combo) * 0.5;
}

/**
 * Score pairwise compatibility for a full combination set.
 * Wrapper exposing SKAI2_calculateSetCompatibilityScore for the combination builder.
 */
function SKAI2_scorePairCompatibilityForSet($combo, $drawHistory, $gameRules)
{
    return SKAI2_calculateSetCompatibilityScore($combo, $drawHistory, $gameRules);
}

/**
 * Validate the game rules spec.
 * (Alias - already implemented as SKAI2_validateGameRules)
 */

// ========================================================
// [[SKAI2_VALIDATION_ABLATION]]
// Section 14: Module ablation and single-module baselines.
// All ablation functions call SKAI2_calculateFinalNumberScores with zeroed-out
// caps for modules not under test. No simplified scoring paths are used.
// ========================================================

/**
 * Run walk-forward validation with only one module active (all others zeroed).
 * Used by compareAgainst*Only() functions and SKAI2_runModuleAblation().
 */
function SKAI2_runSingleModuleValidation($drawHistory, $gameRules, $activeModule, $testSize = 20)
{
    $defaults   = SKAI2_getDefaultSignalCaps();
    $ablatedCaps = [];
    foreach ($defaults as $mod => $cap) {
        $ablatedCaps[$mod] = ($mod === $activeModule) ? $cap : 0.0;
    }
    $params = ['caps' => $ablatedCaps];
    return SKAI2_runWalkForwardValidation($drawHistory, $gameRules, $params, $testSize);
}

/**
 * Compare engine performance using only the frequency module.
 */
function SKAI2_compareAgainstFrequencyOnly($drawHistory, $gameRules, $testSize = 20)
{
    $folds   = SKAI2_runSingleModuleValidation($drawHistory, $gameRules, 'frequency', $testSize);
    $summary = SKAI2_calculateValidationSummary($folds, $gameRules);
    return array_merge($summary, ['baseline_type' => 'frequency_only']);
}

/**
 * Compare engine performance using only the skip module.
 */
function SKAI2_compareAgainstSkipOnly($drawHistory, $gameRules, $testSize = 20)
{
    $folds   = SKAI2_runSingleModuleValidation($drawHistory, $gameRules, 'skip', $testSize);
    $summary = SKAI2_calculateValidationSummary($folds, $gameRules);
    return array_merge($summary, ['baseline_type' => 'skip_only']);
}

/**
 * Compare engine performance using only the recency module.
 */
function SKAI2_compareAgainstRecencyOnly($drawHistory, $gameRules, $testSize = 20)
{
    $folds   = SKAI2_runSingleModuleValidation($drawHistory, $gameRules, 'recency', $testSize);
    $summary = SKAI2_calculateValidationSummary($folds, $gameRules);
    return array_merge($summary, ['baseline_type' => 'recency_only']);
}

/**
 * Compare engine performance using only the pairwise module.
 */
function SKAI2_compareAgainstPairwiseOnly($drawHistory, $gameRules, $testSize = 20)
{
    $folds   = SKAI2_runSingleModuleValidation($drawHistory, $gameRules, 'pairwise', $testSize);
    $summary = SKAI2_calculateValidationSummary($folds, $gameRules);
    return array_merge($summary, ['baseline_type' => 'pairwise_only']);
}

/**
 * Run module ablation study: measure how much each module contributes.
 * Runs the full engine first, then removes each module one at a time.
 * All validation uses SKAI2_calculateFinalNumberScores - no proxy scorers.
 */
function SKAI2_runModuleAblation($drawHistory, $gameRules, $testSize = 20)
{
    // Full engine baseline
    $fullFolds   = SKAI2_runWalkForwardValidation($drawHistory, $gameRules, [], $testSize);
    $fullSummary = SKAI2_calculateValidationSummary($fullFolds, $gameRules);

    $defaults = SKAI2_getDefaultSignalCaps();
    $ablation = [];

    foreach (array_keys($defaults) as $mod) {
        // Zero out only this module
        $caps         = $defaults;
        $caps[$mod]   = 0.0;
        $params       = ['caps' => $caps];
        $folds        = SKAI2_runWalkForwardValidation($drawHistory, $gameRules, $params, $testSize);
        $summary      = SKAI2_calculateValidationSummary($folds, $gameRules);

        $ablation[$mod] = [
            'recall_at_20_without' => $summary['recall_at_20'],
            'recall_at_20_full'    => $fullSummary['recall_at_20'],
            'contribution'         => $fullSummary['recall_at_20'] - $summary['recall_at_20'],
        ];
    }

    return [
        'full_engine' => $fullSummary,
        'ablation'    => $ablation,
    ];
}

/**
 * CSRF token check helper.
 * Call this before any POST/AJAX write action in SKAI 2.0 handlers.
 * Returns true on valid token, false otherwise.
 * Caller must reject the request if false is returned.
 */
function SKAI2_checkCsrfToken()
{
    try {
        return \Joomla\CMS\Session\Session::checkToken('post');
    } catch (\Exception $e) {
        return false;
    }
}

/**
 * Validate that the current user is logged in.
 * Returns true if a logged-in user exists, false otherwise.
 */
function SKAI2_checkLoggedIn()
{
    try {
        $user = \Joomla\CMS\Factory::getApplication()->getIdentity();
        return ($user && $user->id > 0);
    } catch (\Exception $e) {
        return false;
    }
}

 * [[SKAI2_CHANGELOG]] - Verification pass additions
 * - Fixed SKAI2_calculateConditionalSkip: O(n^2) -> O(n) using precomputed last-seen index
 * - Fixed SKAI2_calculateFinalNumberScores: respects $params['caps'] for module ablation
 * - Added defined('_JEXEC') or die security guard
 * - Added SKAI2_checkCsrfToken() and SKAI2_checkLoggedIn() helpers
 * - Added Section 7 named sub-functions: calculateConditionalSkipHitRate,
 *   calculateSkipAdjustment, calculateSkipConfidence
 * - Added Section 8 named sub-functions: calculateRecencyWindowScore,
 *   calculateMultiWindowStability, compareRecentToLongTerm,
 *   calculateStabilityAdjustment
 * - Added Section 9 named sub-functions: calculatePairwiseStats,
 *   calculatePairwiseLift, applyPairwiseShrinkage, calculatePairwiseConfidence,
 *   calculateSetCompatibilityScore
 * - Added Section 13 functions: scoreCombinationDiversity,
 *   scorePairCompatibilityForSet
 * - Added Section 14 functions: runSingleModuleValidation,
 *   compareAgainstFrequencyOnly, compareAgainstSkipOnly,
 *   compareAgainstRecencyOnly, compareAgainstPairwiseOnly, runModuleAblation
 */

/**
 * [[SKAI2_ELITE_FIX_PASS]] - Elite math and learning correction pass
 * - Fixed conditional skip smoothing: numerator uses $hits not $cases
 *   (using $cases assumed all cases were hits, inflating smoothed rate)
 * - Fixed frequency smoothing: uses game-specific base probability as prior mean
 *   (previous formula pulled toward 1/poolSize instead of pick/poolSize)
 * - Added SKAI2_combinationCount() - exact integer C(n,k) via multiplicative formula
 * - Added SKAI2_harmonicNumber() - exact H_N for MRR derivation
 * - Fixed SKAI2_calculateHitAtLeastOneProbability(): exact hypergeometric formula
 *   1 - C(N-M,K)/C(N,K) replacing independence approximation 1-(1-p)^K
 * - Fixed SKAI2_calculateExpectedRandomMRR(): E[MRR] = H_N/N (exact)
 *   replacing incorrect pick/(poolSize+1) which incorrectly scaled with pick
 * - Fixed SKAI2_calculateFinalNumberScores(): cross-domain component normalization.
 *   Raw adjustments for each signal are now min-max normalized to [-1,+1] across
 *   ALL domain numbers before blending. Signal caps are now RELATIVE to base
 *   probability (max_adj = baseProb * cap_fraction) preventing any signal from
 *   contributing an absolute adjustment larger than the base itself.
 * - Added SKAI2_MIN_LEARNING_IMPROVEMENT constant (0.001)
 * - Fixed Gate 7 (SKAI2_validateLearningUpdate): now computes per-fold improvement
 *   over random baseline and requires CI lower >= SKAI2_MIN_LEARNING_IMPROVEMENT.
 *   Previous comparison of raw recall CI lower >= 0.0 always passed (recall >= 0).
 * - Fixed SKAI2_proposeLearningUpdate(): loads past reconciled learning events,
 *   computes per-module help/hurt rates, adjusts caps by +/-0.01 step when evidence
 *   is clear. Returns no_change when history is insufficient.
 * - Upgraded SKAI2_buildBalancedCombinations(): uses scored_numbers from params,
 *   computes real number_score_avg, pair_score, risk_score, explanation per combo.
 *   Deterministic mode supported. Previous version output zeros for all these fields.
 * - Added SKAI2_calculateDailyDigitSmoothedFrequency() - per-position digit frequency
 * - Added SKAI2_calculateDailyDigitConditionalSkip() - per-position digit skip
 * - Added SKAI2_calculateDailyDigitPositionScores() - full digit scoring per position
 * - Added SKAI2_buildDailyDigitPredictions() - position-aware output for Pick 2/3/4/5
 * - Added SKAI2_calculateBonusNumberScores() - full bonus ball scoring with correct
 *   Bayesian smoothing using bonus base probability as prior
 * - Added SKAI2_attachBonusToCombinations() - attaches bonus predictions to combos
 * - Fixed SKAI2_buildBonusPredictions(): delegates to calculateBonusNumberScores()
 * - All changes preserve ASCII-only source, UTF-8 without BOM, PHP 8.1,
 *   Joomla 5.1.2, Sourcerer, ES5, existing save/dashboard/AJAX compatibility
 */

/**
 * [[SKAI2_ELITE_FIX_VERIFICATION]] - Final verification and repair pass
 *
 * Section 1 - Skip smoothing: verified $hits used in numerator in all 3 call sites
 *   (calculateConditionalSkipHitRate, calculateConditionalSkip, calculateDailyDigitConditionalSkip).
 *   No formula using $cases as hit count exists anywhere in the file.
 *   Skip adjustment can be positive, neutral, or negative.
 *   No gambler's fallacy language. Skip capped. Cannot dominate alone.
 *
 * Section 2 - Frequency smoothing: verified game-specific base probability used as prior.
 *   Formula: ($rawCount + $alpha * $expectedRate) / max(1, $totalDraws + $alpha).
 *   No old uniform-prior $smoothedTotal pattern remains.
 *   Adjustment relative to expectedRate, capped, confidence-weighted.
 *
 * Section 3 - Exact random baseline: verified SKAI2_combinationCount and
 *   SKAI2_harmonicNumber helpers exist. SKAI2_calculateHitAtLeastOneProbability
 *   uses exact hypergeometric 1-C(N-M,K)/C(N,K). SKAI2_calculateExpectedRandomMRR
 *   returns H_N/N. No fixed 0.025 or any other hard-coded baseline in active code.
 *
 * Section 4 - Component normalization: verified SKAI2_calculateFinalNumberScores
 *   uses 3-pass architecture: (1) collect raw adjustments, (2) normalize each
 *   component vector to [-1,+1] via SKAI2_normalizeComponentScores, (3) blend
 *   with relative caps (max_adj = baseProb * cap_fraction).
 *
 * Section 5 - Signal caps: verified all caps are relative to base probability.
 *   Example: for base 0.07 and skip cap 0.15, max skip adjustment ~= 0.0105.
 *   Caps applied after normalization. Low confidence reduces effective cap.
 *
 * Section 6 - Learning proposal: verified SKAI2_proposeLearningUpdate loads
 *   reconciled learning events, computes per-module help/hurt rates, adjusts
 *   caps by +/-0.01 step when evidence is clear (>60% of events). Returns
 *   no_change when history is insufficient. Reasons stored in cap_changes.
 *
 * Section 7 - Confidence gate: verified Gate 7 computes per-fold improvement
 *   over random baseline (foldRecall - randomRecall20), bootstraps CI on
 *   improvements, passes only when CI.lower >= SKAI2_MIN_LEARNING_IMPROVEMENT.
 *   Raw recall >= 0.0 comparison removed.
 *
 * Section 8 - Combination builder: verified SKAI2_buildBalancedCombinations
 *   uses scored_numbers and draw_history from params. Computes real
 *   number_score_avg, pair_score, risk_score, explanation. Deterministic mode
 *   uses sorted pool offset instead of random shuffle.
 *
 * Section 9 - Daily digit: verified position-aware functions exist and route
 *   correctly (calculateDailyDigitSmoothedFrequency, calculateDailyDigitConditionalSkip,
 *   calculateDailyDigitPositionScores, buildDailyDigitPredictions).
 *   Skip smoothing uses $hits not $cases. No unordered combo logic for digit games.
 *
 * Section 10 - Bonus ball: verified SKAI2_calculateBonusNumberScores uses
 *   bonus_pick/bonus_pool_size as base probability prior. Main and bonus pools
 *   never mix. SKAI2_attachBonusToCombinations attaches bonus to extra field only.
 *
 * Section 11 - Snapshot consistency: #__skai_run_log is canonical run log.
 *   Migrations add missing columns. #__skai_prediction_snapshots handled via
 *   SKAI2_saveLegacyCompatiblePrediction bridge. Dashboard compatibility preserved.
 *
 * Section 12 - Same engine: all prediction, learning, and validation paths use
 *   SKAI2_calculateFinalNumberScores. No proxy scorers in active code paths.
 *
 * Section 13 - Language: no hype language, no "guaranteed", no "due to hit",
 *   no "overdue". Honest wording throughout user-facing strings.
 *
 * Section 14 - ASCII/UTF-8/ES5/PHP/Joomla: php -l passes. ASCII-only source.
 *   UTF-8 without BOM. No ES6+ constructs (no let/const/arrow/template literal).
 *   PHP 8.1 and Joomla 5.1.2 compatible.
 *
 * Section 15 - Security: _JEXEC guard, CSRF checks on writes, admin/login gates,
 *   sanitized game_id and run_uuid, Joomla SQL API used throughout, output escaped.
 *
 * - Verified skip smoothing uses hits, not cases
 * - Verified frequency smoothing uses game-specific base probability prior
 * - Verified random baseline math is game-specific
 * - Verified component vectors are normalized before blending
 * - Verified signal caps are relative to base probability
 * - Verified learning proposal adjusts caps from evidence
 * - Verified learning confidence gate compares improvement, not raw recall against zero
 * - Verified combination builder uses scored numbers and set-quality metrics
 * - Verified daily digit scoring is position-aware
 * - Verified bonus-ball scoring is separated and integrated
 * - Verified same SKAI2 engine path is used for prediction, learning, and validation
 * - Verified ASCII-only source, UTF-8 without BOM, ES5, Joomla, PHP 8.1, and Sourcerer compatibility
 */

/**
 * Calculation [[SKAI2_VALIDATION_ENGINE]]
 * ----------------------------------------
 * See SKAI2_runWalkForwardValidation and related functions below.
 */

/**
 * Conditional skip analysis - O(n) implementation.
 * Uses a running last-seen index to determine the skip value before each draw
 * without array_slice/re-scan (avoids the O(n^2) cost of the naive approach).
 *
 * Skip does NOT mean "overdue = due".
 * It means: "when numbers were in this skip state before, what happened next?"
 *
 * @param int   $num         Number to analyze
 * @param array $drawHistory Array of draw objects with 'main' key
 * @param array $gameRules   Normalized game rules from SKAI2_getGameRules()
 * @return array Structured skip result with adjustment and label
 */
function SKAI2_calculateConditionalSkip($num, $drawHistory, $gameRules)
{
    $n           = count($drawHistory);
    $currentSkip = SKAI2_calculateCurrentSkip($num, $drawHistory);
    $bucket      = SKAI2_bucketSkipValue($currentSkip);
    $baseRate    = SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($gameRules);

    $cases = 0;
    $hits  = 0;

    // Seed: check whether num appeared in draw[0]
    $isHit0      = isset($drawHistory[0]['main'])
                && is_array($drawHistory[0]['main'])
                && in_array($num, $drawHistory[0]['main'], true);
    $lastSeenIdx = $isHit0 ? 0 : -1;

    // Process draws 1..n-1 (matching the original start-at-1 behavior)
    for ($i = 1; $i < $n; $i++) {
        // skipBefore = skip of $num in drawHistory[0..$i-1]:
        //   length of subHistory = $i
        //   last seen at $lastSeenIdx (0-indexed), or never (-1)
        $skipBefore = ($lastSeenIdx < 0) ? $i : ($i - 1 - $lastSeenIdx);

        $draw  = $drawHistory[$i];
        $isHit = isset($draw['main']) && is_array($draw['main'])
               && in_array($num, $draw['main'], true);

        if (SKAI2_bucketSkipValue($skipBefore) === $bucket) {
            $cases++;
            if ($isHit) {
                $hits++;
            }
        }

        if ($isHit) {
            $lastSeenIdx = $i;
        }
    }

    $conditionalHitRate = ($cases > 0) ? (float)$hits / (float)$cases : $baseRate;
    $alpha              = 1.0;
    // Correct Bayesian smoothing: numerator uses $hits (actual successes), not $cases.
    // Using $cases here would assume every case was a hit, pulling smoothed rate toward 1.
    $smoothedHitRate    = ((float)$hits + $alpha * $baseRate) / ((float)$cases + $alpha);

    $adjustment = SKAI2_calculateSkipAdjustment($smoothedHitRate, $baseRate);
    $confidence = SKAI2_calculateSkipConfidence($cases);

    if ($cases < 5) {
        $label = 'insufficient_data';
    } elseif ($adjustment > 0.02) {
        $label = 'positive';
    } elseif ($adjustment < -0.02) {
        $label = 'negative';
    } else {
        $label = 'neutral';
    }

    return [
        'current_skip'         => $currentSkip,
        'skip_bucket'          => $bucket,
        'conditional_cases'    => $cases,
        'conditional_hits'     => $hits,
        'conditional_hit_rate' => $conditionalHitRate,
        'expected_random_rate' => $baseRate,
        'smoothed_hit_rate'    => $smoothedHitRate,
        'adjustment'           => $adjustment,
        'confidence'           => $confidence,
        'label'                => $label,
    ];
}

/**
 * Multi-window recency and stability analysis.
 * Compares hit rate in short/medium/long windows to base expectation.
 * Recency means: "recent behavior is above, below, or near expectation, with confidence."
 */
function SKAI2_calculateRecencyStability($num, $drawHistory, $gameRules)
{
    $n        = count($drawHistory);
    $baseRate = SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($gameRules);

    $windows = ['short' => 10, 'medium' => 30, 'long' => 100];
    $rates   = [];

    foreach ($windows as $label => $size) {
        $slice = ($n >= $size)
            ? array_slice($drawHistory, $n - $size)
            : $drawHistory;
        $cnt = count($slice);
        $hits = 0;
        foreach ($slice as $draw) {
            if (isset($draw['main']) && is_array($draw['main']) && in_array($num, $draw['main'], true)) {
                $hits++;
            }
        }
        $rates[$label] = ($cnt > 0) ? (float)$hits / (float)$cnt : $baseRate;
    }

    // Stability: 1 - normalized stddev across windows
    $vals = array_values($rates);
    $mean = array_sum($vals) / count($vals);
    $var  = 0.0;
    foreach ($vals as $r) {
        $var += ($r - $mean) * ($r - $mean);
    }
    $stdDev    = sqrt($var / count($vals));
    $stability = 1.0 - min(1.0, $stdDev / max(0.0001, $mean));

    $shortAdj = ($baseRate > 0.0) ? ($rates['short'] - $baseRate) / $baseRate : 0.0;
    $adjustment = max(-0.15, min(0.15, $shortAdj * 0.5));

    $confidence = min(1.0, sqrt((float)min($n, 30) / 30.0)) * $stability;

    return [
        'window_rates'  => $rates,
        'stability'     => $stability,
        'adjustment'    => $adjustment,
        'confidence'    => $confidence,
    ];
}

function SKAI2_calculateLongTermStability($num, $drawHistory, $gameRules)
{
    $n        = count($drawHistory);
    $baseRate = SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($gameRules);

    $windows = [50, 100, 200, 500];
    $rates   = [];

    foreach ($windows as $size) {
        if ($n < $size) {
            continue;
        }
        $slice = array_slice($drawHistory, $n - $size);
        $hits  = 0;
        foreach ($slice as $draw) {
            if (isset($draw['main']) && is_array($draw['main']) && in_array($num, $draw['main'], true)) {
                $hits++;
            }
        }
        $rates[(string)$size] = (float)$hits / (float)$size;
    }

    if (empty($rates)) {
        return ['stability' => 0.5, 'rates' => [], 'long_term_lift' => 0.0];
    }

    $vals  = array_values($rates);
    $mean  = array_sum($vals) / count($vals);
    $var   = 0.0;
    foreach ($vals as $r) {
        $var += ($r - $mean) * ($r - $mean);
    }
    $stability = 1.0 - min(1.0, sqrt($var / count($vals)) / max(0.0001, $mean));

    $longTermLift = ($baseRate > 0.0) ? ($mean - $baseRate) / $baseRate : 0.0;

    return [
        'stability'      => $stability,
        'rates'          => $rates,
        'long_term_lift' => max(-1.0, min(1.0, $longTermLift)),
    ];
}

/**
 * Pairwise compatibility adjustment for a number against a candidate set.
 * Influence capped at 10% unless evidence is strong.
 */
function SKAI2_calculatePairwiseCompatibility($num, $candidateSet, $drawHistory, $gameRules)
{
    if (empty($candidateSet) || empty($drawHistory)) {
        return 0.0;
    }

    $baseRate  = SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($gameRules);
    $totalAdj  = 0.0;
    $pairCount = 0;

    foreach ($candidateSet as $partner) {
        if ((int)$partner === (int)$num) {
            continue;
        }

        $together = 0;
        $numHits  = 0;

        foreach ($drawHistory as $draw) {
            if (!isset($draw['main']) || !is_array($draw['main'])) {
                continue;
            }
            $hasNum     = in_array($num, $draw['main'], true);
            $hasPartner = in_array($partner, $draw['main'], true);
            if ($hasNum) {
                $numHits++;
            }
            if ($hasNum && $hasPartner) {
                $together++;
            }
        }

        $n = count($drawHistory);
        if ($n < 10 || $numHits < 3) {
            continue;
        }

        $expectedPair = $baseRate * (float)$numHits;
        $alpha        = 1.0;
        $shrunkLift   = ($together + $alpha * $expectedPair) / ($numHits + $alpha) / max(0.0001, $baseRate);
        $rawAdj       = ($shrunkLift - 1.0) * 0.05;
        $totalAdj    += $rawAdj;
        $pairCount++;
    }

    if ($pairCount === 0) {
        return 0.0;
    }

    $avgAdj = $totalAdj / $pairCount;
    return max(-0.10, min(0.10, $avgAdj));
}

function SKAI2_rankNumbers($scoredNumbers)
{
    usort($scoredNumbers, function ($a, $b) {
        return ($b['score_total'] <=> $a['score_total']);
    });
    $rank = 1;
    foreach ($scoredNumbers as &$entry) {
        $entry['rank'] = $rank++;
    }
    unset($entry);
    return $scoredNumbers;
}

/**
 * Main SKAI 2.0 scoring function.
 * Scores all numbers in the game domain.
 * Every number starts equal (base probability); adjustments are small and confidence-weighted.
 *
 * Architecture (3 passes + normalization):
 *
 * Pass 1: Collect raw adjustment signals for ALL numbers.
 *
 * Normalize: For each component (frequency, skip, recency, stability), normalize
 * the raw adjustment vector across ALL candidate numbers using min-max normalization
 * to [-1, +1]. This ensures no single number can dominate due to numeric scale, and
 * all components are on the same scale before blending.
 *
 * Pass 2: Apply normalized, RELATIVE adjustments.
 * Signal caps are relative to base probability, not absolute:
 *   max_adjustment = baseProb * cap_fraction
 * Example: for baseProb = 0.07 and skip cap = 0.15, max skip adjustment = 0.0105.
 * This prevents skip or frequency from adding an adjustment larger than the base itself.
 *
 * Pass 3: Pairwise adjustment is computed against top-half candidates and also
 * applied as a relative adjustment (baseProb * pairwise_cap).
 *
 * drawHistory: array of draws, each with 'main' (int[]) and optionally 'extra' (int[]).
 * Returns array of scored number objects.
 */
function SKAI2_calculateFinalNumberScores($drawHistory, $gameRules, $params = [])
{
    $domain     = SKAI2_buildNumberDomain($gameRules);
    $baseProb   = SKAI2_calculateBaseProbability($gameRules);
    $sampleSize = count($drawHistory);

    // Merge caller-supplied caps (used by module ablation) over defaults.
    $caps = (isset($params['caps']) && is_array($params['caps']))
        ? array_merge(SKAI2_getDefaultSignalCaps(), $params['caps'])
        : SKAI2_getDefaultSignalCaps();

    // Use shared thresholds to avoid duplication with calculateEvidenceGrade
    $gt = SKAI2_getSampleSizeGradeThresholds();
    if ($sampleSize <= $gt['E_max']) {
        $grade  = 'E';
        $reason = 'Fewer than 10 draws available';
    } elseif ($sampleSize <= $gt['D_max']) {
        $grade  = 'D';
        $reason = 'Fewer than 30 draws of training data';
    } elseif ($sampleSize <= $gt['C_max']) {
        $grade  = 'C';
        $reason = 'Fewer than 100 draws of training data';
    } elseif ($sampleSize <= $gt['B_max']) {
        $grade  = 'B';
        $reason = 'Fewer than 300 draws of training data';
    } else {
        $grade  = 'A';
        $reason = 'Sufficient training data (300+ draws)';
    }

    // --- Pass 1: Collect raw signals for all numbers ---
    $rawSignals = [];
    $rawFreqAdj   = [];
    $rawSkipAdj   = [];
    $rawRecencyAdj = [];
    $rawStabilityAdj = [];

    foreach ($domain as $num) {
        $freqData    = SKAI2_calculateSmoothedFrequency($num, $drawHistory, $gameRules);
        $skipData    = SKAI2_calculateConditionalSkip($num, $drawHistory, $gameRules);
        $recencyData = SKAI2_calculateRecencyStability($num, $drawHistory, $gameRules);
        $ltData      = SKAI2_calculateLongTermStability($num, $drawHistory, $gameRules);
        $avgConf     = ($freqData['confidence'] + $skipData['confidence'] + $recencyData['confidence']) / 3.0;

        // Raw adjustment signals (unitless, not yet capped or scaled)
        $rawFreqAdj[$num]      = (float)$freqData['adjustment'];
        $rawSkipAdj[$num]      = (float)$skipData['adjustment'];
        $rawRecencyAdj[$num]   = (float)$recencyData['adjustment'];
        $rawStabilityAdj[$num] = (float)($ltData['long_term_lift'] * $ltData['stability']);

        $rawSignals[$num] = [
            'freq_data'    => $freqData,
            'skip_data'    => $skipData,
            'recency_data' => $recencyData,
            'lt_data'      => $ltData,
            'avg_conf'     => $avgConf,
        ];
    }

    // --- Normalize each component vector across the domain ---
    // SKAI2_normalizeComponentScores maps values to [-1, +1] using min-max.
    // This ensures no number can dominate due to raw scale differences.
    $normFreq      = SKAI2_normalizeComponentScores($rawFreqAdj);
    $normSkip      = SKAI2_normalizeComponentScores($rawSkipAdj);
    $normRecency   = SKAI2_normalizeComponentScores($rawRecencyAdj);
    $normStability = SKAI2_normalizeComponentScores($rawStabilityAdj);

    // --- Pass 2: Blend normalized signals with RELATIVE caps ---
    // Signal cap is applied as a fraction of base probability so that adjustments
    // are always proportional. A cap of 0.15 means the maximum adjustment from that
    // signal is baseProb * 0.15, never +0.15 in absolute terms.
