<section class="skai-section skai-section--insights" id="skai-insights-section" aria-label="Today's SKAI insights" style="display:none">
  <!-- Hidden by default; revealed after SKAI Auto-Tune + ML prediction complete -->
  <div class="skai-story-label">Key Findings</div>
  <p class="skai-section-orient">This section surfaces the strongest signals from this run. Review the pattern intelligence and ranked picks below before saving or acting.</p>
  <div class="skai-findings-block">
  <div class="skai-card skai-card--hero">
    <h2 class="skai-h2">What SKAI found today</h2>
    <p class="skai-hero-subline">
      SKAI analyzed your recent draw history, auto-tuned its settings, and ranked numbers by how often they hit, how much pressure is building, and how their behavior is shifting.
    </p>
    <ul class="skai-hero-chips">
      <li class="skai-hero-chip">Reads number behavior: stable vs. volatile</li>
      <li class="skai-hero-chip">Highlights numbers with strong recent signals</li>
      <li class="skai-hero-chip">Shows why certain picks move to the top</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <!-- SKAI: Pattern Intelligence Summary (Act 1 bridge) -->
  <div class="skai-card skai-pi" role="region" aria-label="Interpreting the Signals">
    <h3 class="skai-h3">Interpreting the Signals</h3>

    <!-- CHG: Plain-language legend with inline tooltip anchors for statistical education -->
    <p class="skai-pi-legend">
      <strong>How SKAI reads the board:</strong>
      <span class="skai-tooltip-anchor">
        <strong>Stable</strong>
        <span class="skai-tooltip-icon" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="What does Stable mean?" data-skai-tip="A Stable number appears at a consistent cadence. Its skip count stays close to the historical average, making it a reliable anchor for your selection.">i</span>
      </span> numbers show up at a steady rhythm and act like anchors.
      <span class="skai-tooltip-anchor">
        <strong>Volatile</strong>
        <span class="skai-tooltip-icon" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="What does Volatile mean?" data-skai-tip="A Volatile number hits in clusters then goes quiet. Its skip count swings above and below average -- useful when it's in an active streak.">i</span>
      </span> numbers move in bursts -- they can heat up quickly, then cool off.
      <span class="skai-tooltip-anchor">
        <strong>Reset</strong>
        <span class="skai-tooltip-icon" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="What does Reset mean?" data-skai-tip="A Reset number just appeared after a long absence. Statistically, recently-hit numbers often take a short rest before returning -- SKAI monitors this cooling period.">i</span>
      </span> numbers just hit after a long quiet stretch and often pause.
      <span class="skai-tooltip-anchor">
        <strong>Transition</strong>
        <span class="skai-tooltip-icon" role="button" tabindex="0" aria-label="What does Transition mean?" data-skai-tip="A Transition number is shifting from one behavior pattern to another. SKAI flags it because behavioral changes can signal an upcoming frequency shift.">i</span>
      </span> numbers are changing behavior; SKAI watches these for the next move.
    </p>

    <div class="skai-pi-grid">
      <!-- MAIN BALLS -->
      <div class="skai-pi-card">
        <h4 class="skai-pi-title">Main numbers</h4>

        <?php
          $mCounts     = $piMain['counts'] ?? ['stable'=>0,'volatile'=>0,'reset'=>0,'transition'=>0];
          $mStable     = (int)($mCounts['stable'] ?? 0);
          $mVolatile   = (int)($mCounts['volatile'] ?? 0);
          $mReset      = (int)($mCounts['reset'] ?? 0);
          $mTransition = (int)($mCounts['transition'] ?? 0);
          $mTotal      = max(1, $mStable + $mVolatile + $mReset + $mTransition);

          // Simple "mood" line for the main field
          $mainMood = 'Patterns are fairly balanced across the main board.';
          if ($mVolatile > $mTransition && $mVolatile >= $mStable) {
            $mainMood = 'The main board is running hot and streaky -- volatile numbers are doing most of the talking.';
          } elseif ($mTransition >= $mVolatile && $mTransition >= $mStable) {
            $mainMood = 'Many main numbers are in a transition phase -- behavior is shifting and SKAI expects changes.';
          } elseif ($mStable >= $mVolatile && $mStable >= $mTransition) {
            $mainMood = 'The main board is relatively steady -- more numbers are behaving like reliable anchors.';
          }

          // "How to use this board" guidance for main numbers
          $mainTip = 'A common approach is to start with 1-2 stable anchor numbers and then layer in a few hot or transitioning picks.';
          if ($mStable && !$mVolatile && !$mTransition) {
            $mainTip = 'Tonight looks calm on the main board. Many players would lean more on stable anchors and use your wheeling system to spread around them.';
          } elseif ($mVolatile > $mStable && $mVolatile >= $mTransition) {
            $mainTip = 'Because volatility is high, some players like to keep 1-2 stable anchors and give extra room in their wheels to a small set of hot, volatile numbers.';
          } elseif ($mTransition > $mStable && $mTransition > $mVolatile) {
            $mainTip = 'With many numbers in transition, it can help to mix anchors with a handful of numbers in motion that SKAI flags as shifting.';
          }
        ?>

        <p class="skai-pi-counts">
          Stable: <strong><?php echo $mStable; ?></strong> &middot;
          Volatile: <strong><?php echo $mVolatile; ?></strong> &middot;
          Reset: <strong><?php echo $mReset; ?></strong> &middot;
          Transition: <strong><?php echo $mTransition; ?></strong>
        </p>
        <p class="skai-pi-mood"><?php echo htmlspecialchars($mainMood, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?></p>
        <p class="skai-pi-tip"><?php echo htmlspecialchars($mainTip, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?></p>

        <?php if (!empty($piMain['top'])): ?>
          <p class="skai-pi-ex">Main numbers SKAI is watching most closely:</p>
          <ul class="skai-pi-list">
            <?php foreach (['stable','volatile','reset','transition'] as $k):
              $arr = $piMain['top'][$k] ?? [];
              if (!$arr) continue;

              // Only show a few per line so it's readable
              $arr = array_slice($arr, 0, 4);

              $labelNice = ucfirst($k);
              $prefix = '';
              if ($k === 'stable') {
                $prefix = 'Steady anchors that keep showing up: ';
              } elseif ($k === 'volatile') {
                $prefix = 'Numbers that tend to hit in bursts when they wake up: ';
              } elseif ($k === 'reset') {
                $prefix = 'Recently hit after a long wait -- they may cool for a bit: ';
              } elseif ($k === 'transition') {
                $prefix = 'Numbers whose behavior is shifting -- SKAI is watching these: ';
              }

              // Turn each entry into "61 (around 9.2% of recent draws)"
              $parts = array_map(function($x){
                $lab = $x['label'] ?? (string)($x['n'] ?? '');
                if ($lab === '') return '';
                if (isset($x['prob'])) {
                  $pt  = round(100 * (float)$x['prob'], 1);
                  return $lab . ' (around ' . $pt . '% of recent draws)';
                }
                return $lab;
              }, $arr);
              $parts = array_filter($parts);
              if (!$parts) continue;
            ?>
              <li>
                <em><?php echo htmlspecialchars($labelNice, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>.</em>
                <?php echo htmlspecialchars($prefix, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>
                <?php echo htmlspecialchars(implode('; ', $parts), ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>.
              </li>
            <?php endforeach; ?>
          </ul>
        <?php endif; ?>
      </div>

      <!-- EXTRA BALLS (if present) -->
      <?php if (!empty($piExtra)): ?>
      <div class="skai-pi-card">
        <h4 class="skai-pi-title">Extra / bonus</h4>

        <?php
          $eCounts     = $piExtra['counts'] ?? ['stable'=>0,'volatile'=>0,'reset'=>0,'transition'=>0];
          $eStable     = (int)($eCounts['stable'] ?? 0);
          $eVolatile   = (int)($eCounts['volatile'] ?? 0);
          $eReset      = (int)($eCounts['reset'] ?? 0);
          $eTransition = (int)($eCounts['transition'] ?? 0);
          $eTotal      = max(1, $eStable + $eVolatile + $eReset + $eTransition);

          $extraMood = 'Bonus numbers are fairly balanced today.';
          if ($eVolatile > $eTransition && $eVolatile >= $eStable) {
            $extraMood = 'Bonus numbers are behaving aggressively -- more of them are in a hot, volatile phase.';
          } elseif ($eTransition >= $eVolatile && $eTransition >= $eStable) {
            $extraMood = 'Bonus numbers are in a transition phase -- SKAI expects shifts in which ones are active.';
          } elseif ($eStable >= $eVolatile && $eStable >= $eTransition) {
            $extraMood = 'Bonus numbers are relatively steady; there are clear anchor candidates here too.';
          }

          $extraTip = 'Many players like to pick one strong bonus anchor and then rotate around a small set of hot or transitioning bonus numbers.';
          if ($eStable && !$eVolatile && !$eTransition) {
            $extraTip = 'With mostly stable bonus numbers, the analysis suggests picking an anchor-like candidate and spreading remaining picks across the pool.';
          } elseif ($eVolatile > $eStable && $eVolatile >= $eTransition) {
            $extraTip = 'Because volatility is high in the bonus field, consider giving a bit more space to the hot bonus picks SKAI is flagging.';
          } elseif ($eTransition > $eStable && $eTransition > $eVolatile) {
            $extraTip = 'With many bonus numbers in transition, it can help to mix one anchor with a rotating cast of numbers in motion.';
          }
        ?>

        <p class="skai-pi-counts">
          Stable: <strong><?php echo $eStable; ?></strong> &middot;
          Volatile: <strong><?php echo $eVolatile; ?></strong> &middot;
          Reset: <strong><?php echo $eReset; ?></strong> &middot;
          Transition: <strong><?php echo $eTransition; ?></strong>
        </p>
        <p class="skai-pi-mood"><?php echo htmlspecialchars($extraMood, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?></p>
        <p class="skai-pi-tip"><?php echo htmlspecialchars($extraTip, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?></p>

        <?php if (!empty($piExtra['top'])): ?>
          <p class="skai-pi-ex">Bonus numbers SKAI is watching:</p>
          <ul class="skai-pi-list">
            <?php foreach (['stable','volatile','reset','transition'] as $k):
              $arr = $piExtra['top'][$k] ?? [];
              if (!$arr) continue;

              $arr = array_slice($arr, 0, 4);

              $labelNice = ucfirst($k);
              $prefix = '';
              if ($k === 'stable') {
                $prefix = 'Steady bonus anchors: ';
              } elseif ($k === 'volatile') {
                $prefix = 'Hot / streaky bonus picks: ';
              } elseif ($k === 'reset') {
                $prefix = 'Recently hit after a quiet stretch: ';
              } elseif ($k === 'transition') {
                $prefix = 'Bonus numbers in motion (watch for moves): ';
              }

              $parts = array_map(function($x){
                $lab = $x['label'] ?? (string)($x['n'] ?? '');
                if ($lab === '') return '';
                if (isset($x['prob'])) {
                  $pt  = round(100 * (float)$x['prob'], 1);
                  return $lab . ' (around ' . $pt . '% of recent draws)';
                }
                return $lab;
              }, $arr);
              $parts = array_filter($parts);
              if (!$parts) continue;
            ?>
              <li>
                <em><?php echo htmlspecialchars($labelNice, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>.</em>
                <?php echo htmlspecialchars($prefix, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>
                <?php echo htmlspecialchars(implode('; ', $parts), ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>.
              </li>
            <?php endforeach; ?>
          </ul>
        <?php endif; ?>
      </div>
      <?php endif; ?>
    </div>

    <!-- Advice / narrative from SKAI -->
    <div class="skai-pi-adv" role="note" aria-live="polite">
      <p class="skai-pi-head">

<!-- [[skai-learning-faq]] Post-run FAQ: Understanding how SKAI learns -->
<div id="skai-learning-faq" role="region" aria-label="Understanding how SKAI learns">
  <div class="skai-faq__head">
    <p class="skai-faq__head-label">Lottery intelligence</p>
    <h3 class="skai-faq__head-title">Understanding how SKAI learns</h3>
  </div>
  <div class="skai-faq__item">
    <button type="button" class="skai-faq__btn" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="skai-faq-panel-1" id="skai-faq-btn-1">
      Does SKAI learn from everyone&#8217;s favorite numbers?
      <span class="skai-faq__icon" aria-hidden="true">+</span>
    </button>
    <div id="skai-faq-panel-1" class="skai-faq__panel" role="region" aria-labelledby="skai-faq-btn-1">
      <p>No. SKAI learns from lottery-specific draw history and engine evidence &#8212; not from the numbers other users save or prefer. Crowd superstition does not enter the shared engine. What gets refined is how the engine reads draw patterns, calibrates score ranges, and adjusts for near-boundary candidates.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="skai-faq__item">
    <button type="button" class="skai-faq__btn" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="skai-faq-panel-2" id="skai-faq-btn-2">
      If many people run Powerball, what actually improves?
      <span class="skai-faq__icon" aria-hidden="true">+</span>
    </button>
    <div id="skai-faq-panel-2" class="skai-faq__panel" role="region" aria-labelledby="skai-faq-btn-2">
      <p>The shared Powerball engine can accumulate more run-level evidence: better parameter summaries, more calibration history, and stronger tail recovery signals derived from Powerball&#8217;s own draws. User picks are not treated as prediction truth &#8212; they are not used to teach the engine what numbers to prefer.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="skai-faq__item">
    <button type="button" class="skai-faq__btn" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="skai-faq-panel-3" id="skai-faq-btn-3">
      What do my profile and strategy actually change?
      <span class="skai-faq__icon" aria-hidden="true">+</span>
    </button>
    <div id="skai-faq-panel-3" class="skai-faq__panel" role="region" aria-labelledby="skai-faq-btn-3">
      <p><strong>Balanced / Wider Search / Safer Range</strong> affect how bold or restrained the run behaves &#8212; how willing SKAI is to reach for high-variance candidates versus staying close to long-term behavior.</p>
      <p><strong>AI-focused / Balanced / Pattern-focused</strong> affect how the system combines model-scoring emphasis with skip-pattern signal emphasis. These do not change what the core engine learned &#8212; they shape the current run and how the final set is assembled.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="skai-faq__item">
    <button type="button" class="skai-faq__btn" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="skai-faq-panel-4" id="skai-faq-btn-4">
      Does this mean every user gets the same picks?
      <span class="skai-faq__icon" aria-hidden="true">+</span>
    </button>
    <div id="skai-faq-panel-4" class="skai-faq__panel" role="region" aria-labelledby="skai-faq-btn-4">
      <p>No. Users share the same lottery-level engine, but final outputs differ based on profile selection, strategy selection, blend ratio, and personal set construction. Two users running the same lottery with different profiles will receive different recommendation sets even if the shared engine state is identical.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="skai-faq__item">
    <button type="button" class="skai-faq__btn" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="skai-faq-panel-5" id="skai-faq-btn-5">
      How do I know what was active on my run?
      <span class="skai-faq__icon" aria-hidden="true">+</span>
    </button>
    <div id="skai-faq-panel-5" class="skai-faq__panel" role="region" aria-labelledby="skai-faq-btn-5">
      <p>The &#8220;What powered this run&#8221; panel shown after your run completes lists every active component: profile, strategy, blend, draws analyzed, shared learning state, calibration, and tail recovery. The live engine status chips in the teaching panel also update as each component activates.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="skai-faq__item">
    <button type="button" class="skai-faq__btn" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="skai-faq-panel-6" id="skai-faq-btn-6">
      What is the benefit of this live implementation?
      <span class="skai-faq__icon" aria-hidden="true">+</span>
    </button>
    <div id="skai-faq-panel-6" class="skai-faq__panel" role="region" aria-labelledby="skai-faq-btn-6">
      <p>More continuity from run to run as the lottery-specific engine accumulates evidence. Better lottery-specific refinement over time through calibration and tail recovery. Clearer visibility into exactly what SKAI applied. Less guesswork about what the engine is doing in the background &#8212; now surfaced directly on the page. Statistical guidance, not guarantees.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

  </div><!-- /skai-findings-block -->
</section>

<div class="skai-divider"></div>

<?php if ($skaiUiVisible): ?>
<section class="skai-advanced skai-mode-smart-up" role="region" aria-label="Supporting analytical views within the SKAI system">
  <div class="skai-story-label skai-story-label--muted" style="margin-bottom:6px;">Supporting Analytical Views</div>
  <h2 class="skai-h2">Supporting Number Dynamics</h2>
  <div class="skai-ref-intro">
    <strong>Reference data:</strong> These tables provide supporting context before and after a run.
    Use them to understand number behavior alongside the primary recommendation panel above, not in place of it.
  </div>
  <p class="skai-advanced-subline">
    These views help you understand <strong>number behavior</strong> in a simple, consistent way:
    how often a number has hit, how long it has been skipping, what phase it appears to be in,
    and SKAI&rsquo;s estimated chance it will hit on the next draw.
  </p>

  <p class="skai-advanced-subline skai-advanced-subline--note">
    <strong>Chance to hit next</strong> is the plain-English name for <strong>P(hit|k)</strong> -- an estimate based on what happened historically when a number reached the same skip value.
    The <strong>Pattern</strong> label (Reset / Transition / Volatile) is a behavioral summary, not a promise.
  </p>

  <!-- Education: Pattern labels explained (this renders inside the Advanced Analysis section) -->
  <details class="skai-help" aria-labelledby="help-patterns-title">
    <summary id="help-patterns-title" class="skai-help-summary">
      What do the Pattern labels mean (Reset, Transition, Volatile)?
    </summary>

    <div class="skai-help-body">
      <p>
        SKAI assigns each number a <strong>pattern label</strong> by comparing its <em>current skip</em>
        (how many draws it has missed since it last appeared) to its own historical rhythm.
        These labels do <strong>not</strong> predict outcomes -- they describe what the number is doing <em>right now</em>.
      </p>

      <h4>Reset</h4>
      <p>
        <strong>Reset</strong> means the number appeared in the most recent draw, so its skip is now <strong>0</strong>.
      </p>
      <p>
        <em>How to interpret it:</em> This simply identifies numbers coming off a hit.
        Some numbers cluster, others pause -- the label is descriptive, not a signal to chase.
      </p>

      <h4>Transition</h4>
      <p>
        <strong>Transition</strong> is the middle zone: the number is neither freshly hit nor in an extended skip state.
        It is behaving within a typical range, but could move toward either Reset (hits) or Volatile (longer skip).
      </p>
      <p>
        <em>How to interpret it:</em> Use this as a "watch" label -- rely more on the probability column and your preferences.
      </p>

      <h4>Volatile</h4>
      <p>
        <strong>Volatile</strong> means the number is skipping <em>longer than usual</em> compared to its own historical gap between hits.
        It is in a more uneven phase (streaky behavior).
      </p>
      <p>
        <em>How SKAI detects this:</em> the current skip is meaningfully above the number&rsquo;s typical (median) gap.
      </p>
      <p>
        <em>How to interpret it:</em> Volatile does <strong>not</strong> mean "due."
        It means the number is outside its normal rhythm -- sometimes it resolves soon, sometimes it extends.
      </p>

      <p>
        <strong>Best practice:</strong> Read <strong>Pattern</strong> together with <strong>Chance to hit next (P(hit|k))</strong>.
        Pattern describes behavior; probability estimates likelihood.
      </p>
    </div>
  </details>


  <!-- CANONICAL Main Skip & Hit block (collapsible, collapsed by default) -->
  <details class="skai-skiphit skai-skiphit--main skai-details-collapsible" aria-label="Skip &amp; Hit - Main Balls">
    <summary class="skai-details-summary">
      <h3 class="skai-h3">Skip &amp; Hit - Main Balls</h3>
      <span class="skai-details-caret" aria-hidden="true"></span>
    </summary>
    <div class="skai-details-content">
      <p class="skai-reset-note">
        <strong>-Reset- (yellow)</strong> means that number just hit in the latest draw, so its skip counter has gone back to 0.
      </p>

      <?php if (!empty($mainSkipHitRows)): ?>
      <table class="skai-table skai-sortable" role="table" aria-live="polite">
        <caption class="skai-visually-hidden">Skip and Hit metrics for main balls</caption>
        <thead>
          <tr>
            <th scope="col" data-key="label"   aria-sort="none">Ball</th>
            <th scope="col" data-key="skip"    aria-sort="none">Skip</th>
            <th scope="col" data-key="hits"    aria-sort="none">Hits</th>
            <th scope="col" data-key="pattern" aria-sort="none">Pattern</th>
            <th scope="col" data-key="prob" aria-sort="descending"
                title="Chance this number will hit in the next draw, based on how long it has been skipping.">
              Chance to hit next (P(hit|k))
            </th>
            <th scope="col" data-key="support" aria-sort="none">Support</th>
            <th scope="col"
                data-key="cooc"
                aria-sort="none"
                title="How often this ball has appeared in the same draw as SKAI&rsquo;s stronger numbers over the last <?php echo (int)$coK; ?> draws.">
              Partner hits (<?php echo (int)$coK; ?>)
            </th>
          </tr>
        </thead>
        <tbody>
          <?php foreach ($mainSkipHitRows as $row): ?>
            <tr>
              <td><?php echo htmlspecialchars($row['label'], ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?></td>
              <td><?php echo (int)$row['skip']; ?></td>
              <td><?php echo (int)$row['hits']; ?></td>

              <td
                class="<?php echo htmlspecialchars($row['pattern_class'] ?? ('pi-' . strtolower((string)($row['pattern'] ?? 'transition'))), ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>"
                title="<?php echo htmlspecialchars($row['title'] ?? '', ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>"
                aria-label="<?php echo htmlspecialchars($row['aria_label'] ?? ($row['title'] ?? ''), ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>">
                <?php echo htmlspecialchars($row['pattern'] ?? 'Transition', ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>
              </td>

              <td><?php echo htmlspecialchars($row['prob_t'], ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?></td>
              <td><?php echo (int)$row['hitk']; ?>/<?php echo (int)$row['sup']; ?></td>
              <td><?php echo isset($row['cooc']) ? (int)$row['cooc'] : 0; ?></td>
            </tr>
          <?php endforeach; ?>
        </tbody>
      </table>
      <?php else: ?>
        <p class="skai-empty" role="note">No main-ball data available.</p>
      <?php endif; ?>
    </div>
  </details>

<?php if (!empty($hasExtra)): ?>
  <div class="skai-skiphit skai-skiphit--extra" role="region" aria-label="Skip &amp; Hit - Extra Balls">
  <?php /* keep this block OPEN; the closing </div> and corresponding endif; appear after the Extra table below */ ?>

    <!-- SKAI: Auto-Tune Results consolidated into Live Run Activity card -->

    <!-- SKAI Pulse Orb moved to SKAI Hybrid Intelligence panel -->

    <?php if (!empty($skaiAutoTune) && !empty($skaiAutoTune['best'])): ?>
      <?php
        $autoBest    = $skaiAutoTune['best'];
        $autoCands   = $skaiAutoTune['candidates'] ?? [];
        $autoW       = isset($autoBest['windows'])   ? (int) $autoBest['windows']   : 0;
        $autoK       = isset($autoBest['laplace_k']) ? (int) $autoBest['laplace_k'] : 0;
        $autoAvg     = isset($autoBest['avg_score']) ? (float) $autoBest['avg_score'] : 0.0;
        $autoHitRate = isset($autoBest['hit_rate'])  ? (float) $autoBest['hit_rate']  : 0.0;

        // Estimate how many configurations were evaluated.
        // This mirrors the ranges already used by skai_autotuneRollingBacktest.
        $autoConfigsTested = 0;
        if (!empty($KsArr)) {
          $autoSpan = max(0, (int) floor(($winMax - $winMin) / max(1, $winStep)) + 1);
          $autoConfigsTested = $autoSpan * count($KsArr);
        } else {
          // Fallback: use visible candidates count if KsArr is not available.
          $autoConfigsTested = count($autoCands);
        }
      ?> 
      <div class="skai-bt skai-bt--autotune-summary" role="note" aria-label="SKAI Auto-Tune Results" style="display:none;margin-bottom:12px">
        <div class="skai-bt-summary skai-bt-summary--hero">

<?php /*
<!-- SKAI: Rolling Backtest (SKAI Performance) - hidden here; canonical backtest lives in Act 1 -->
<div class="skai-bt" role="region" aria-label="SKAI Backtest Performance">
	  <p class="skai-backtest-label">How SKAI Skip &amp; Hit compares to random 20-number wheels (historical)</p>
	  <h3 class="skai-h3">SKAI Skip &amp; Hit Backtest - Proof of Performance</h3>
	  <p class="skai-backtest-subline">
	    These results reflect how the Skip &amp; Hit core of SKAI performed on past draws using the same Skip &amp; Hit settings you used for your prediction today. The window W shown below is the auto-tuned history depth selected for this lottery, and it can be different for each game.
	  </p>

	  <?php if (!empty($skaiBacktest)): ?>
		<?php
      $btWindows = (int)($skaiBacktest['windows'] ?? 0);
      $btK       = isset($skaiBacktest['laplace_k']) ? (int)$skaiBacktest['laplace_k'] : null;
      $btAvg     = (float)($skaiBacktest['avg_score'] ?? 0.0);
      $btHit1    = (float)($skaiBacktest['hit_rate_main_ge1'] ?? 0.0);
      $btMeanM   = (float)($skaiBacktest['mean_main_matches'] ?? 0.0);
      $btMeanX   = (float)($skaiBacktest['mean_extra_matches'] ?? 0.0);
      $btPick    = (int)($skaiBacktest['pick_size'] ?? 0);
      $btHasExtra= !empty($skaiBacktest['has_extra']);

      $wMain = isset($skaiBacktest['weights']['main_hit']) ? (int)$skaiBacktest['weights']['main_hit'] : 0;
      $wExtra= isset($skaiBacktest['weights']['extra_hit']) ? (int)$skaiBacktest['weights']['extra_hit'] : 0;

      // Baseline: how a simple random wheel (same pick size) would behave on this game (theoretical).
      $randMeanMain    = null;
      $randHit1        = null;
      $randLiftHit1    = null;

      // Monte Carlo baseline: simulate random wheels on the same backtest draws.
      $mcRandMeanMain  = null;
      $mcRandHit1      = null;
      $mcRandLiftHit1  = null;
      $mcTrials        = 256; // number of random wheels per draw for simulation

      if (isset($mainMax) && $mainMax > 0 && $btPick > 0) {
          // Estimate how many main balls are drawn in this lottery (e.g. 5 for Powerball).
          $drawMainCount = 0;
          if (!empty($mainDraws)) {
              $sample = reset($mainDraws);
              if (is_array($sample)) {
                  $drawMainCount = count($sample);
              } else {
                  $parts = preg_split('/[^\d]+/', (string) $sample, -1, PREG_SPLIT_NO_EMPTY);
                  $drawMainCount = count($parts);
              }
          }

          if ($drawMainCount > 0) {
              // Expected main matches for random picks:
              //   E[intersection size] = P * D / N
              //   P = pick size (your SKAI wheel size)
              //   D = main balls drawn in the game
              //   N = main domain (e.g. 69 for Powerball)
              $randMeanMain = ($btPick * $drawMainCount) / $mainMax;

              // Probability of hitting at least 1 main ball with random picks.
              // Exact without-replacement model:
              //   P(no hit) = prod_{j=0}^{D-1} (N - P - j) / (N - j)
              //   P(hit >= 1) = 1 - P(no hit)
              $N = $mainMax;
              $P = min($btPick, $N);
              $D = min($drawMainCount, $N);

              $noHit = 1.0;
              for ($j = 0; $j < $D; $j++) {
                  $num = max(0.0, $N - $P - $j);
                  $den = max(1.0, $N - $j);
                  $noHit *= ($den > 0.0) ? ($num / $den) : 1.0;
              }

              $randHit1 = 1.0 - $noHit;

              // Lift against random (theoretical baseline).
              if ($randHit1 > 0.0) {
                  $randLiftHit1 = $btHit1 / $randHit1;
              }

              // Monte Carlo simulation over the same backtest draws.
              if (!empty($skaiBacktest['series'])) {
                  $series        = $skaiBacktest['series'];
                  $domain        = range(1, $mainMax);
                  $sumMatches    = 0;
                  $sumHit1       = 0;
                  $validDraws    = 0;

                  foreach ($series as $row) {
                      if (empty($row['actual_main']) || !is_array($row['actual_main'])) {
                          continue;
                      }
                      $validDraws++;
                  }

                  if ($validDraws > 0) {
                      for ($t = 0; $t < $mcTrials; $t++) {
                          foreach ($series as $row) {
                              if (empty($row['actual_main']) || !is_array($row['actual_main'])) {
                                  continue;
                              }
                              $actual = array_map('intval', $row['actual_main']);
                              if (!$actual) {
                                  continue;
                              }

                              // Draw P distinct random numbers from the domain for this trial/draw.
                              // Reproducibility Key -- for consistent test results
                              if (isset($runParams['deterministic']) && $runParams['deterministic']) {
                                  $reproKey = $runParams['reproKey'] ?? $runParams['seed'] ?? null; // Accept both
                                  if ($reproKey !== null) {
                                      mt_srand($reproKey + $i); // Vary key per iteration for diversity
                                  }
                                  $pickKeys = [];
                                  $domainArr = array_values($domain);
                                  for ($j = 0; $j < min($btPick, count($domain)); $j++) {
                                      $idx = mt_rand(0, count($domainArr) - 1);
                                      $pickKeys[] = $domainArr[$idx];
                                      array_splice($domainArr, $idx, 1);
                                  }
                              } else {
                                  $pickKeys = (array) array_rand($domain, min($btPick, count($domain)));
                              }
                              $randPick = [];
                              foreach ($pickKeys as $k) {
                                  $randPick[] = $domain[$k];
                              }

                              $matches = count(array_intersect($randPick, $actual));
                              $sumMatches += $matches;
                              if ($matches > 0) {
                                  $sumHit1++;
                              }
                          }
                      }

                      $totalSim       = max(1, $validDraws * $mcTrials);
                      $mcRandMeanMain = $sumMatches / $totalSim;
                      $mcRandHit1     = $sumHit1 / $totalSim;

                      if ($mcRandHit1 > 0.0) {
                          $mcRandLiftHit1 = $btHit1 / $mcRandHit1;
                      }
                  }
              }
          }
      }

      // Compact random baseline values for UI tiles (prefer Monte Carlo when available).
      $randMeanForUi = ($mcRandMeanMain !== null) ? $mcRandMeanMain : $randMeanMain;
      $randHitForUi  = ($mcRandHit1   !== null) ? $mcRandHit1   : $randHit1;
      $haveRandomUi  = ($randMeanForUi !== null && $randHitForUi !== null);

    ?>

    <!-- Premium summary panel -->
    <div class="skai-bt-summary skai-bt-summary--hero" role="note" aria-live="polite">
      <div class="skai-bt-summary-main">
        <p class="skai-bt-kv">
          <strong>Window (history depth):</strong>
          W=<?php echo $btWindows; ?>
          <?php if ($btK !== null): ?>
            &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp;<strong>Laplace K:</strong> <?php echo $btK; ?>
          <?php endif; ?>
        </p>
        <p class="skai-bt-kv">
          <strong>Historical performance score:</strong>
          <?php echo number_format($btAvg, 2); ?>
          (main - <?php echo $wMain; ?><?php if ($btHasExtra): ?>, extra - <?php echo $wExtra; ?><?php endif; ?>)
        </p>
        <p class="skai-bt-kv">
          <strong>At least 1 main hit:</strong>
          <?php echo number_format($btHit1 * 100.0, 1); ?>%
        </p>
      </div>

       <div class="skai-bt-summary-metrics">
         <div class="skai-bt-metric">
           <span class="skai-bt-label">Mean main matches</span>
           <span class="skai-bt-value"><?php echo number_format($btMeanM, 2); ?></span>
         </div>
         <?php if ($btHasExtra): ?>
           <div class="skai-bt-metric">
             <span class="skai-bt-label">Mean extra matches</span>
             <span class="skai-bt-value"><?php echo number_format($btMeanX, 2); ?></span>
           </div>
         <?php endif; ?>
         <div class="skai-bt-metric">
           <span class="skai-bt-label">Pick size</span>
           <span class="skai-bt-value"><?php echo $btPick; ?></span>
         </div>
       </div>

       <?php if ($haveRandomUi): ?>
         <div class="skai-bt-summary-comparison" role="group" aria-label="Random versus Skip &amp; Hit averages">
           <h4 class="skai-bt-subheading">Random vs Skip &amp; Hit (per draw)</h4>
           <table class="skai-table skai-bt-mini">
             <thead>
               <tr>
                 <th scope="col">Metric</th>
                 <th scope="col">Random</th>
                 <th scope="col">Skip &amp; Hit</th>
               </tr>
             </thead>
             <tbody>
               <tr>
                 <td>Mean main matches</td>
                 <td><?php echo number_format($randMeanForUi, 2); ?></td>
                 <td><?php echo number_format($btMeanM, 2); ?></td>
               </tr>
               <tr>
                 <td>At least 1 main hit</td>
                 <td><?php echo number_format($randHitForUi * 100.0, 1); ?>%</td>
                 <td><?php echo number_format($btHit1 * 100.0, 1); ?>%</td>
               </tr>
             </tbody>
           </table>
         </div>
       <?php endif; ?>

       <p class="skai-bt-note">
         These stats show how the Skip &amp; Hit core of SKAI performed when it replayed your history on past draws using the current Skip &amp; Hit settings.
         <?php if ($randMeanMain !== null && $randHit1 !== null): ?>
           By calculation, picking <?php echo (int) $btPick; ?> numbers completely at random for each draw
           would typically average about <?php echo number_format($randMeanMain, 2); ?> main hits and
           hit at least one main ball about <?php echo number_format($randHit1 * 100.0, 1); ?>% of the time.
         <?php endif; ?>
         <?php if ($mcRandMeanMain !== null && $mcRandHit1 !== null): ?>
           In a Monte Carlo simulation with <?php echo (int) $mcTrials; ?> random wheels per draw over the same history,
           pure random picks actually averaged about <?php echo number_format($mcRandMeanMain, 2); ?> main hits and
           hit at least one main ball about <?php echo number_format($mcRandHit1 * 100.0, 1); ?>% of the time.
         <?php endif; ?>
         On that same history, this Skip &amp; Hit setup averaged <?php echo number_format($btMeanM, 2); ?> main hits and
         hit at least one main ball <?php echo number_format($btHit1 * 100.0, 1); ?>% of the time
         across the same draws<?php
           $liftToUse = $mcRandLiftHit1 !== null ? $mcRandLiftHit1 : $randLiftHit1;
           if ($liftToUse !== null): ?>, roughly
           <?php echo number_format($liftToUse, 1); ?>x the hit at least 1 main rate of pure random picks<?php endif; ?>.
         This backtest currently evaluates the Skip &amp; Hit core only; it does not yet include the additional machine learning layer SKAI uses for live predictions. Backtest results are not a guarantee for future draws; they are a statistical comparison that shows how much better this tuned Skip &amp; Hit configuration has performed than simple random wheels on your past data. Random baselines are computed from standard lottery probability formulas plus Monte Carlo simulations that anyone can reproduce.
       </p>
    </div>

    <?php
      // Last 10 draws: SKAI pick vs actual results (proof of performance)
      $btSeries = $skaiBacktest['series'] ?? [];
      $btTail   = array_slice($btSeries, -10);

    <?php if (!empty($btTail)): ?>

          $lotteryConfig['lotteryConfig']['num_extra_balls_drawn']
          ?? $lotteryConfig['lotteryConfig']['extra_ball_count']
          ?? $lotteryConfig['lotteryConfig']['extraCount']
          ?? $lotteryConfig['num_extra_balls_drawn']
          ?? $lotteryConfig['extra_ball_count']
          ?? $lotteryConfig['extraCount']
          ?? 2
        );
        if ($defaultExtraPickCount <= 0) { $defaultExtraPickCount = 2; }
      ?>
      <div class="skai-save-controls">
        <label class="skai-label" for="skai-pick-main">How many main numbers?</label>
        <input id="skai-pick-main" class="skai-input" type="number" min="1" max="20" value="<?php echo (int)$defaultPickSize; ?>" />
        <?php if ($hasExtraLocal): ?>
          <label class="skai-label" for="skai-pick-extra">How many extra balls?</label>
          <input id="skai-pick-extra" class="skai-input" type="number" min="0" max="<?php echo (int)$defaultExtraPickCount; ?>" value="1" />
        <?php endif; ?>
      </div>

      <label class="skai-label" for="skai-notes">Notes (optional)</label>
      <textarea id="skai-notes" name="notes" rows="2" class="skai-textarea" placeholder="Add a short note-"></textarea>

      <div class="skai-save-actions">

        <button type="submit" id="skai-submit-save" class="skai-btn skai-btn--primary" disabled>Save to My Dashboard</button>
      </div>
    </form>

  </div>
<?php else: ?>

  <!-- Education: What is Laplace K? -->
  <details class="skai-help" aria-labelledby="help-k-title">
    <summary id="help-k-title" class="skai-help-summary">What is Laplace K?</summary>
    <div class="skai-help-body">
      <p><strong>Laplace K</strong> is a tiny 'guard rail' that prevents zero or tiny sample sizes from making our probability estimates (<code>P(hit|k)</code>) too extreme.</p>
      <ul>
        <li><strong>K = 0</strong> (no smoothing): uses raw counts only. Best when you have lots of data and want maximum sensitivity - but it can overreact to noise.</li>
        <li><strong>K = 1</strong> (default): gentle smoothing. Safer for typical lottery histories; reduces overfitting without hiding real signals.</li>
        <li><strong>K = 2</strong> (extra smoothing): more conservative. Helpful when data is sparse or very volatile, but it may mute short-term spikes.</li>
      </ul>
      <p><em>Rule of thumb:</em> Try K = 1 first. If results swing too much between windows, test K = 2. If your history is long and stable, test K = 0.</p>
    </div>
  </details>

<!-- Education: What is P(hit|k)? -->
<details class="skai-help" aria-labelledby="help-phitk-title">
  <summary id="help-phitk-title" class="skai-help-summary">What is P(hit|k)?</summary>
  <div class="skai-help-body">
    <p><strong>P(hit|k)</strong> means the chance this number hits on the next draw, given its current <em>skip</em> is <code>k</code>. A number&rsquo;s <em>skip</em> is how many draws it has missed since it last appeared.</p>
    <p>We estimate it from past draws at the same skip value using Laplace smoothing:</p>
    <pre><code>P(hit|k) = (hits_at_skip_k + K) / (trials_at_skip_k + 2K)</code></pre>
    <ul>
      <li><code>hits_at_skip_k</code>: how many times a number did hit when its skip was exactly <code>k</code></li>
      <li><code>trials_at_skip_k</code>: how many times we observed numbers with skip <code>k</code></li>
      <li><code>K</code>: Laplace K (0/1/2). K = 1 (default) gently avoids over/under-confidence with small samples.</li>
     </ul>
    <p><em>How to read it:</em> higher P(hit|k) means more likely to hit next, all else equal. We still consider <strong>skip</strong> and <strong>pattern</strong> to avoid chasing noise.</p>
  </div>
</details>

<!-- Education: What do the Skip & Hit patterns mean? -->
<details class="skai-help" aria-labelledby="help-patterns-title">
  <summary id="help-patterns-title" class="skai-help-summary">
    What do the Skip &amp; Hit patterns mean?
  </summary>

  <div class="skai-help-body">
    <p>
      SKAI groups numbers into simple <strong>behavior patterns</strong> based on how their current
      <em>skip</em> compares to their own historical rhythm. These labels do <strong>not</strong>
      predict outcomes -- they describe how a number is behaving <em>right now</em> relative to its past.
    </p>

    <h4>Reset</h4>
    <p>
      A number labeled <strong>Reset</strong> has just appeared in the most recent draw.
      Its skip count is now <strong>0</strong>.
    </p>
    <p>
      <em>How to read it:</em> Resets often pause briefly after hitting, but some numbers do cluster.
      SKAI flags these so you can see which numbers are coming off a hit -- not to chase them blindly.
    </p>

    <h4>Volatile</h4>
    <p>
      <strong>Volatile</strong> numbers are skipping <em>longer than usual</em> compared to their
      typical gap between hits. In other words, they are running late relative to their own history.
    </p>
    <p>
      <em>How SKAI detects this:</em> The current skip is significantly larger than the number&rsquo;s
      historical median gap.
    </p>
    <p>
      <em>How to read it:</em> Volatile does <strong>not</strong> mean &ldquo;due.&rdquo;
      It means the number is in an uneven phase -- sometimes these resolve quickly, sometimes they stretch further.
    </p>

    <h4>Transition</h4>
    <p>
      <strong>Transition</strong> numbers sit between stable and volatile behavior.
      They are neither freshly hit nor in an extended skip state.
    </p>
    <p>
      <em>How to read it:</em> Transition is a watch zone.
      These numbers are changing state, and SKAI monitors them closely for movement in either direction.
    </p>

    <p>
      <strong>Important:</strong> Pattern labels are meant to be read <em>alongside</em>
      <strong>Chance to hit next (P(hit|k))</strong>, not instead of it.
      Patterns describe behavior; probabilities estimate likelihood.
    </p>
  </div>
</details>

<?php endif; ?>  <!-- end disabled Skip & Hit save section -->

    // JSON_HEX_TAG ensures </script> cannot appear unescaped; JSON_HEX_AMP/APOS/QUOT prevent HTML injection.
    // Do not apply htmlspecialchars here -- it corrupts JSON strings containing & or < characters.
    echo $skaiEvidencePayloadJson;
  ?></script>
 
<?php endif; ?>

             <td
              class="<?php echo htmlspecialchars($row['pattern_class'] ?? ('pi-' . strtolower((string)($row['pattern'] ?? 'transition'))), ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?>"

            </td>

            <td><?php echo (int)$row['hitk']; ?>/<?php echo (int)$row['sup']; ?></td>
            <td><?php echo isset($row['cooc']) ? (int)$row['cooc'] : 0; ?></td>
          </tr>
        <?php endforeach; ?>
      </tbody>
    </table>
    <?php else: ?>

</div>
</section>

$heroLotteryNameNorm = strtoupper(trim((string) $heroLotteryName));
$gameIdNorm          = strtoupper(trim((string) $gameId));

$scopeLabel = '';

  'LOTTOAMERICA',

  'CASH_4_LIFE'
];

  'EURO MILLIONS'
];

  'EUROMILLIONS',
  'EURO_MILLIONS',
  'EURO-MILLIONS'
];

  in_array($heroLotteryNameNorm, $multiCountryLotteryNames, true) ||
  in_array($gameIdNorm, $multiCountryGameIds, true)
) {
  $scopeLabel = 'Multi-Country';
} elseif (
  in_array($heroLotteryNameNorm, $multiStateLotteryNames, true) ||
  in_array($gameIdNorm, $multiStateGameIds, true)
) {
  $scopeLabel = 'Multi-State';
}

if ($scopeLabel !== '') {
  $stateName = $scopeLabel;
} else {
  // Priority order:
  // 1. Explicit state/region name variable
  // 2. URL param stn
  // 3. URL param state
  // 4. URL param st
  // 5. Final fallback from DB suffix

  if (isset($stateNameFull) && trim((string) $stateNameFull) !== '') {
    $stateName = (string) $stateNameFull;
  } elseif (isset($_GET['stn']) && trim((string) $_GET['stn']) !== '') {

  } elseif (isset($dbCol) && trim((string) $dbCol) !== '') {
    // Fallback: derive region from draw table name suffix
    $dbColStr = strtoupper((string) $dbCol);

    $abbr = '';

    if (preg_match('/[_-]([A-Z]{2})\s*$/', $dbColStr, $m)) {
      $abbr = $m[1];

    }

    if ($abbr !== '') {

        // US

    }
  }
}
// Build aligned H1 + SEO structure: AI system first, prediction second, methods supporting.

  ? ($stateName . ' ' . $heroLotteryName . ' Adaptive AI Prediction System')

It learns from draw behavior, refines probability structures, and applies multi-layer analysis including skip-hit dynamics, frequency modeling, and pattern optimization.';

);

  if (is_array($last) && !empty($last['date'])) {

}
if ($rawLastDrawDate === '' && isset($mainHistory) && is_array($mainHistory) && !empty($mainHistory)) {

// Prefer existing draw-date variables if present
if (isset($lastDrawDate) && trim((string)$lastDrawDate) !== '') {

<div class="skai-system-explainer skai-mode-smart-up" role="region" aria-label="Designed for clarity, not guesswork">
  <p class="skai-system-explainer__kicker">Designed for clarity, not guesswork</p>
  <h2 class="skai-system-explainer__title">How SKAI frames this run</h2>
  <p class="skai-system-explainer__body">
    SKAI does not rely on isolated signals or static formulas. It evaluates multiple dimensions of lottery behavior and continuously refines its models based on observed outcomes. This page shows both the primary AI-ranked output and the supporting analytical context so you can understand what the system is doing, not just what it produced.
  </p>

<section class="skai-quick-answer skai-mode-smart-up" aria-label="SKAI output interpretation guide">
  <p class="skai-quick-answer__kicker">How SKAI learns on this page</p>
  <ul class="skai-quick-answer__list">
    <li><strong>System role:</strong> adaptive AI prediction system built for this lottery, not a static calculator.</li>
    <li><strong>What the rankings mean:</strong> higher-ranked sets scored stronger against this run's active evidence, not guaranteed future outcomes.</li>
    <li><strong>What influenced this run:</strong> draw-history freshness, profile, strategy, blend, calibration, tail recovery, and reproducibility mode.</li>
    <li><strong>Where skip-hit fits:</strong> as a supporting behavioral layer inside the broader SKAI learning framework.</li>
    <li><strong>What to do next:</strong> run SKAI, review the rationale, compare supporting analytical views, and save the set you want to track in My LottoExpert.</li>
  </ul>
</section>

    <div id="skai-batch-detail-body" style="font-size:0.85rem;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word;max-height:220px;overflow-y:auto;"></div>
    <button type="button" id="skai-batch-detail-close" class="btn-outline"
      style="margin-top:8px;font-size:0.82rem;padding:4px 10px;">Close</button>
  </div>

  <!-- Save all / Save selected (post-completion) -->
  <div id="skai-batch-save-row" style="display:none;margin-top:12px;gap:8px;flex-wrap:wrap;">
    <div id="skai-batch-save-completion-note" style="width:100%;font-size:0.85rem;color:#065f46;background:#f0fdf4;border:1px solid #bbf7d0;border-radius:8px;padding:8px 12px;margin-bottom:8px;">
      <strong>All runs complete.</strong> Each run was automatically saved to your dashboard as it finished.
      Use <strong>Re-save All</strong> or <strong>Re-save Selected</strong> only if you need to save again.
    </div>
    <button type="button" id="skai-batch-save-all" class="btn"
      style="font-size:0.85rem;padding:6px 14px;">
      Re-save All
    </button>
    <button type="button" id="skai-batch-save-selected" class="btn-outline"
      style="font-size:0.85rem;padding:6px 14px;">
      Re-save Selected
    </button>
    <div id="skai-batch-save-status"
      role="status" aria-live="polite"
      style="display:none;width:100%;font-size:0.88rem;font-weight:600;color:#0A1A33;padding:6px 0 2px 0;">
    </div>
    <!-- Engine-Optimized Blend legend: shown only when at least one row was optimized -->
    <div id="skai-blend-opt-legend"
      style="display:none;width:100%;font-size:0.78rem;color:#92400e;margin-top:2px;">
      * Blend adjusted by Engine-Optimized Blend
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
<?php endif; ?>

<!-- SKAI: How this works - help panel (always visible) -->
<details id="skai-how-it-works" class="skai-mode-smart-up"
  style="margin:14px 0 0 0;border:1px solid rgba(28,102,255,0.18);border-radius:12px;padding:0;background:#fff;">
  <summary style="padding:12px 16px;font-weight:700;font-size:0.95rem;cursor:pointer;list-style:none;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;color:#0A1A33;">
    <span>&#8505;&#xFE0E;&nbsp; About the 9-Combo Batch &mdash; FAQ</span>
    <span style="font-size:0.8rem;font-weight:400;color:#7F8DAA;">Click to expand</span>
  </summary>
  <div style="padding:4px 16px 16px 16px;font-size:0.88rem;line-height:1.65;color:#374151;">
    <p><strong>Generate 9-Run Batch</strong> asks SKAI to do nine separate analysis runs all at once.
    Think of it like asking nine different experts to look at the same lottery and each one uses a slightly different style.
    Some are bold and exploratory. Some are careful and conservative. Some lean on patterns; others lean on the AI engine.
    Together they give you a full picture.</p>
    <p><strong>Why does it take long?</strong> Each run is a complete analysis. SKAI has to look through all the historical draw data,
    run its AI model, and score every number. Nine of those in a row takes some patience. You can stop at any time and keep the results you already have.</p>
    <p><strong>What does the grid mean?</strong> The 3 rows are the three risk styles (Balanced, Wider Search, Safer Range).
    The 3 columns are the three strategy modes (AI-focused, Balanced, Pattern-focused).
    Each cell shows whether that combination is Waiting, Running, or Done.
    When a run finishes, a View link appears so you can look at the picks it produced.</p>
    <p><strong>What does Save All do?</strong> Each run is saved automatically the moment it completes &mdash; you do not need to click anything.
    The Re-save All button is a fallback if you want to save results again after they have already been saved.</p>
    <p><strong>Should I click Save while the batch is still running?</strong> No need &mdash; saves happen automatically after each run.
    A green &#x2713; toast appears after each one so you can see it working.</p>
    <p><strong>What if I stop the batch early?</strong> No problem. Every run that already completed was already saved automatically.
    Use Re-save Selected if you want to save partial results again.</p>
    <p><strong>What happens after saving?</strong> Nothing scary. You stay right here. A small green message appears confirming the save.
    Your picks and all your settings remain visible exactly as they were.</p>
  </div>
</details>

<script>
/* SKAI: Floating help FAB tooltip on first hover */
;(function () {
  "use strict";
  var fab = document.getElementById("skai-help-fab-btn");
  var lbl = document.getElementById("skai-help-fab-label");
  if (!fab || !lbl) { return; }
  fab.addEventListener("mouseover", function () {
    lbl.style.opacity = "1";
  }, false);
  fab.addEventListener("mouseout", function () {
    lbl.style.opacity = "0";
  }, false);
  fab.addEventListener("focus", function () {
    lbl.style.opacity = "1";
  }, false);
  fab.addEventListener("blur", function () {
    lbl.style.opacity = "0";
  }, false);
})();
</script>

<script id="skai-quick-view-layout-js">
/* [[SKAI_QUICK_VIEW_LAYOUT_2026_05_20]] Layout/wording only. No prediction math changes. */
(function(){
  'use strict';
  function q(id){ return document.getElementById(id); }
  function txt(el, value){ if(el){ el.textContent = value; } }
  function after(anchor,node){ if(anchor && node && anchor.parentNode){ if(anchor.nextSibling){ anchor.parentNode.insertBefore(node, anchor.nextSibling); } else { anchor.parentNode.appendChild(node); } } }
  function append(parent,node){ if(parent && node && node.parentNode !== parent){ parent.appendChild(node); } }
  function setAttrs(el, attrs){ if(!el){ return; } for(var k in attrs){ if(Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(attrs,k)){ el.setAttribute(k, attrs[k]); } } }
  function makeLiveHost(){
    var host=q('skai-live-run-activity');
    if(host){ return host; }
    host=document.createElement('section');
    host.id='skai-live-run-activity';
    host.setAttribute('aria-label','Live run activity');
    host.innerHTML='<div class="skai-live-run-head"><div><h2 class="skai-live-run-title">Live Run Activity</h2><p class="skai-live-run-note">Watch SKAI find the best history window, score the number strength, run the batch table when selected, and prepare your final numbers.</p></div><div class="skai-live-run-pill">Live status</div></div>';
    var anchor=q('skai-advanced') || document.querySelector('.hero-cta-wrap') || q('skai-quick-view-card');
    after(anchor, host);
    return host;
  }
  function simplifyLabels(){
    txt(q('startPrediction'), 'Generate Single Run');
    txt(q('skai-batch-launch'), 'Generate 9-Run Batch');
    txt(q('skai-mle-batch-launch-hero'), 'Run Recommended 9-Run Batch');
    txt(q('skai-mle-batch-launch'), 'Run Recommended 9-Run Batch');
    txt(q('skai-batch-confirm'), 'Start 9-Run Batch');
    txt(q('skai-batch-panel-title'), '9-Run Batch Progress');
    txt(q('skai-progress-stage-msg'), 'Ready when you are.');
    txt(q('skai-progress-sub-msg'), 'Choose Single Run or Batch Run to start.');
    var labels=document.querySelectorAll('.skai-progress-label');
    var names=['Finding Window','Loading History','Scoring Main Numbers','Scoring Extra Numbers','Building Picks','Ready'];
    for(var i=0;i<labels.length && i<names.length;i++){ labels[i].textContent=names[i]; }
    var mathTitle=document.querySelector('.skai-math-console-title');
    if(mathTitle){ mathTitle.textContent='Live Math Activity'; }
    var autoTitle=q('skai-progress-autotune-results');
    var at=autoTitle ? autoTitle.querySelector('.skai-progress-autotune-title') : null;
    if(at){ at.textContent='Window Math'; }
    setAttrs(q('startPrediction'), {'aria-label':'Generate a single SKAI run for this lottery'});
    setAttrs(q('skai-batch-launch'), {'aria-label':'Generate a 9-run batch comparison for this lottery'});
  }

  function friendlyText(value){
    return String(value || '')
      .replace(/Auto-Tune/g, 'Window search')
      .replace(/auto-tune/g, 'window search')
      .replace(/Training Main AI/g, 'Scoring main numbers')
      .replace(/Training Extra AI/g, 'Scoring extra numbers')
      .replace(/Training complete/g, 'Numbers ready')
      .replace(/Training/g, 'Scoring')
      .replace(/Run Prediction/g, 'Generate')
      .replace(/Picks ready/g, 'Your numbers are ready')
      .replace(/Analysis complete/g, 'Run complete');
  }
  function normalizeVisibleText(root){
    try{
      var nodes=[];
      var ids=['skai-progress-stage-msg','skai-progress-sub-msg','skai-progress-computation','skai-progress-eta','skai-batch-current-label','skai-batch-progress-text','skai-progress-orb-status','skai-window-status'];
      for(var i=0;i<ids.length;i++){ var el=q(ids[i]); if(el){ nodes.push(el); } }
      if(root && root.nodeType===1){ nodes.push(root); }
      for(var j=0;j<nodes.length;j++){ if(nodes[j] && nodes[j].childNodes.length===1 && nodes[j].firstChild.nodeType===3){ nodes[j].textContent=friendlyText(nodes[j].textContent); } }
    }catch(_){ }
  }
  function watchFriendlyText(){
    try{
      var host=q('skai-live-run-activity') || document.body;
      var obs=new MutationObserver(function(list){
        for(var i=0;i<list.length;i++){
          var m=list[i];
          if(m.type==='characterData' && m.target){ m.target.nodeValue=friendlyText(m.target.nodeValue); }
          if(m.type==='childList'){
            for(var j=0;j<m.addedNodes.length;j++){ normalizeVisibleText(m.addedNodes[j]); }
          }
        }
      });
      obs.observe(host,{subtree:true,childList:true,characterData:true});
      normalizeVisibleText(host);
    }catch(_){ }
  }

  function reflowQuickView(){
    var adv=q('skai-advanced');
    var cta=document.querySelector('.hero-cta-wrap');
    if(adv && cta){ after(cta, adv); }
    var host=makeLiveHost();
    var progress=q('skai-progress-indicator');
    var batch=q('skai-batch-panel');
    append(host, progress);
    append(host, batch);
    var results=q('skai-ai-results');
    if(results){ after(host, results); }
    var advBody=q('skai-adv-body');
    var how=q('skai-how-it-works');
    if(advBody && how){ how.setAttribute('data-skai-advanced-moved','1'); append(advBody, how); }
    var support=document.querySelector('section.skai-advanced');
    if(advBody && support && support.id !== 'skai-advanced'){
      support.setAttribute('data-skai-advanced-moved','1');
      append(advBody, support);
    }
    try{ window.SKAI_FORCE_ADV_COLLAPSE = true; }catch(_){ }
    if(adv){ adv.classList.add('skai-collapsed'); }
    var btn=q('skai-adv-toggle');
    var body=q('skai-adv-body');
    if(btn){ btn.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false'); var span=btn.querySelector('[data-skai-label-text]'); if(span){ span.textContent='Advanced Settings'; } }
    if(body){ body.style.display='none'; }
  }
  function boot(){
    simplifyLabels();
    reflowQuickView();
    var runs=0;
    var timer=window.setInterval(function(){
      runs++;
      simplifyLabels();
      reflowQuickView();
      if(runs>=12){ window.clearInterval(timer); }
    }, 500);
  }
  if(document.readyState === 'loading'){ document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', boot, false); } else { boot(); }
})();
</script>

<script id="skai-qse-layout-js">
/* [[SKAI_QSE_LAYOUT_2026_05_20]] Quick / Strategy / Evidence view polish. Layout and wording only. No prediction math changes. */
(function(){
  'use strict';
  function q(id){return document.getElementById(id);} 
  function qs(sel,root){return (root||document).querySelector(sel);} 
  function after(anchor,node){if(anchor&&node&&anchor.parentNode){anchor.parentNode.insertBefore(node,anchor.nextSibling);}}
  function append(parent,node){if(parent&&node&&node.parentNode!==parent){parent.appendChild(node);}}
  function setText(el,value){if(el){el.textContent=value;}}
  function revealBatchTable(){
    var host=q('skai-live-run-activity');
    var batch=q('skai-batch-panel');
    if(host&&batch&&batch.parentNode!==host){host.appendChild(batch);}
    if(batch){batch.style.display='block'; batch.setAttribute('aria-hidden','false');}
    var text=q('skai-batch-progress-text'); if(text){text.textContent='Ready to run the 9-run batch.';}
    var label=q('skai-batch-current-label'); if(label){label.textContent='Nine SKAI run styles will appear here as they start, finish, and save.';}
    try{if(host&&host.scrollIntoView){host.scrollIntoView({behavior:'smooth',block:'start'});}}catch(_){ }
  }

  function ensureViewTabs(){
    var tabs=q('skai-qse-tabs');
    if(tabs){return tabs;}
    var quick=q('skai-quick-view-card');
    if(!quick){return null;}
    tabs=document.createElement('div');
    tabs.id='skai-qse-tabs';
    tabs.className='skai-qse-tabs';
    tabs.setAttribute('role','group');
    tabs.setAttribute('aria-label','Choose SKAI view');
    tabs.innerHTML='<span class="skai-qse-tabs__label">View:</span>'+
      '<button type="button" class="skai-qse-tab" data-skai-view="quick" aria-pressed="true">Quick</button>'+
      '<button type="button" class="skai-qse-tab" data-skai-view="strategy" aria-pressed="false">Strategy</button>'+
      '<button type="button" class="skai-qse-tab" data-skai-view="evidence" aria-pressed="false">Evidence</button>';
    after(quick,tabs);
    tabs.addEventListener('click',function(e){
      var b=e.target&&e.target.closest?e.target.closest('[data-skai-view]'):null;
      if(!b){return;}
      applyView(b.getAttribute('data-skai-view')||'quick');
    },false);
    return tabs;
  }

  function applyView(view){
    view=(view==='strategy'||view==='evidence')?view:'quick';
    document.body.classList.remove('skai-view-quick','skai-view-strategy','skai-view-evidence');
    document.body.classList.add('skai-view-'+view);
    var tabs=q('skai-qse-tabs');
    if(tabs){
      var buttons=tabs.querySelectorAll('[data-skai-view]');
      for(var i=0;i<buttons.length;i++){buttons[i].setAttribute('aria-pressed',buttons[i].getAttribute('data-skai-view')===view?'true':'false');}
    }
    if(view==='strategy'){document.body.classList.remove('skai-batch-selected');}
    if(view==='evidence'){document.body.classList.remove('skai-single-selected','skai-batch-selected');}
    var adv=q('skai-advanced'), advBody=q('skai-adv-body'), advBtn=q('skai-adv-toggle');
    if(view==='evidence' && adv && advBody){
      adv.classList.remove('skai-collapsed');
      advBody.style.display='block';
      if(advBtn){advBtn.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true');}
    } else if(adv && advBody){
      adv.classList.add('skai-collapsed');
      advBody.style.display='none';
      if(advBtn){advBtn.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false');}
    }
  }

  function ensureRunChoicePanel(){
    var panel=q('skai-run-choice-panel');
    var cta=qs('.hero-cta-wrap');
    if(!panel){
      panel=document.createElement('section');
      panel.id='skai-run-choice-panel';
      panel.className='skai-run-choice-panel';
      panel.setAttribute('aria-label','Choose SKAI run type');
      panel.innerHTML='<p class="skai-run-choice-title">Now choose your run</p><div class="skai-run-choice-actions" id="skai-run-choice-actions"></div>';
      if(cta){after(cta,panel);} else {var quick=q('skai-qse-tabs')||q('skai-quick-view-card'); after(quick,panel);}
    }
    var actions=q('skai-run-choice-actions');
    var single=q('startPrediction');
    var batchWrap=q('skai-mle-precision-batch');
    if(actions && single){append(actions,single);}
    if(actions && batchWrap){batchWrap.classList.add('skai-qse-run-choice'); append(actions,batchWrap);}
    setText(q('startPrediction'),'Generate Single Run');
    setText(q('skai-batch-launch'),'Generate 9-Run Batch');
    setText(q('skai-mle-batch-launch'),'Run Recommended 9-Run Batch');
    setText(q('skai-mle-batch-launch-hero'),'Run Recommended 9-Run Batch');
    if(single && !single.getAttribute('data-qse-single-bound')){
      single.setAttribute('data-qse-single-bound','1');
      single.addEventListener('click',function(){
        document.body.classList.add('skai-single-selected');
        document.body.classList.remove('skai-batch-selected');
        applyView('quick');
      },true);
    }
    var batchBtns=[];
    var b1=q('skai-batch-launch'), b2=q('skai-mle-batch-launch'), b3=q('skai-mle-batch-launch-hero');
    if(b1){batchBtns.push(b1);} if(b2){batchBtns.push(b2);} if(b3){batchBtns.push(b3);}
    for(var bi=0;bi<batchBtns.length;bi++){
      if(!batchBtns[bi].getAttribute('data-qse-batch-bound')){
        batchBtns[bi].setAttribute('data-qse-batch-bound','1');
        batchBtns[bi].addEventListener('click',function(){
          document.body.classList.add('skai-batch-selected');
          document.body.classList.remove('skai-single-selected');
          applyView('quick');
          revealBatchTable();
        },true);
      }
    }
    var confirm=q('skai-batch-confirm');
    if(confirm && !confirm.getAttribute('data-qse-confirm-bound')){
      confirm.setAttribute('data-qse-confirm-bound','1');
      confirm.addEventListener('click',function(){
        document.body.classList.add('skai-batch-selected');
        document.body.classList.remove('skai-single-selected');
        applyView('quick');
        revealBatchTable();
      },true);
    }
    if(actions && !q('skai-qse-single-hint')){
      var hint=document.createElement('p');
      hint.id='skai-qse-single-hint';
      hint.className='skai-qse-single-hint';
      hint.textContent='Choose how SKAI should think first. Then run one focused recommendation or a full 9-run batch comparison.';
      panel.appendChild(hint);
    }
    return panel;
  }

  function compactTrainingOptions(){
    var panel=q('skai-run-choice-panel');
    var opts=q('skai-run-options');
    if(!opts){return;}
    opts.classList.add('skai-qse-compact');
    if(panel&&panel.parentNode){panel.parentNode.insertBefore(opts,panel);} 
    setText(q('skai-run-options-title'),'How should SKAI think today?');
    if(!q('skai-qse-training-note')){
      var p=document.createElement('p');
      p.id='skai-qse-training-note';
      p.className='skai-qse-training-note';
      p.textContent='Choose this before you generate. Balanced is best for most runs; Wider Search explores more; Safer Range stays closer to steady patterns.';
      var title=q('skai-run-options-title');
      if(title&&title.parentNode){title.parentNode.insertBefore(p,title.nextSibling);}
    }
    setText(q('skai-profile-title'), 'Run style');
    var ph=qs('#skai-profile-title .opt-hint'); if(ph){ph.textContent='How bold SKAI should be';}
    var profileHelp=qs('[aria-labelledby="skai-profile-title"] .opt-help-line');
    if(profileHelp){profileHelp.innerHTML='Pick the overall behavior. <strong>Balanced</strong> is best for most users.';}
    setText(q('skai-strategy-title'), 'Number mix');
    var sh=qs('#skai-strategy-title .opt-hint'); if(sh){sh.textContent='AI vs. pattern influence';}
    var strategyHelp=qs('[aria-labelledby="skai-strategy-title"] .opt-help-line');
    if(strategyHelp){strategyHelp.innerHTML='Choose whether SKAI leans AI-focused, pattern-focused, or balanced.';}
    setText(q('skai-blend-title'), 'AI influence');
    var blendHelp=qs('#skai-blend-title + .opt-help-line');
    if(blendHelp){blendHelp.innerHTML='Adjust how much AI influence SKAI uses for this run.';}
  }

  function moveDeepExplanationsToAdvanced(){
    var advBody=q('skai-adv-body');
    if(!advBody){return;}
    var ids=['skai-learning-explainer','skai-mode-explainer','skai-deterministic-card','skai-learn-how','skai-how-it-works'];
    for(var i=0;i<ids.length;i++){var el=q(ids[i]); if(el){append(advBody,el);}}
    if(!q('skai-qse-advanced-note')){
      var note=document.createElement('div');
      note.id='skai-qse-advanced-note';
      note.style.cssText='margin:0 0 12px 0;padding:10px 12px;border-radius:12px;background:#f8fafc;border:1px solid #e5e7eb;color:#334155;font-size:.86rem;line-height:1.45;';
      note.innerHTML='<strong>Advanced details:</strong> deeper run logic, mode behavior, repeatable-run controls, and evidence panels are kept here so Quick stays clean.';
      advBody.insertBefore(note,advBody.firstChild);
    }
  }

  function placeLiveActivity(){
    var host=q('skai-live-run-activity');
    if(!host){return;}
    var panel=q('skai-run-choice-panel');
    if(panel){after(panel,host);} else {var opts=q('skai-run-options'); if(opts){after(opts,host);}}
    var progress=q('skai-progress-indicator');
    var batch=q('skai-batch-panel');
    if(progress){append(host,progress);} 
    if(batch){append(host,batch);} 
    var note=qs('.skai-live-run-note',host);
    if(note){note.textContent='Follow the window search, live math, green progress dots, and batch table as SKAI builds your numbers.';}
  }

  function ensureEvidenceNote(){
    if(q('skai-qse-evidence-note')){return;}
    var tabs=q('skai-qse-tabs');
    if(!tabs){return;}
    var note=document.createElement('div');
    note.id='skai-qse-evidence-note';
    note.className='skai-qse-evidence-note';
    note.innerHTML='<strong>Evidence:</strong> review the deeper run logic, math support, and proof panels without changing the prediction math.';
    after(tabs,note);
  }

  function bootQSE(){
    ensureViewTabs();
    ensureRunChoicePanel();
    compactTrainingOptions();
    moveDeepExplanationsToAdvanced();
    placeLiveActivity();
    ensureEvidenceNote();
    applyView('quick');
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading'){document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',bootQSE,false);} else {bootQSE();}
  var count=0;
  var timer=window.setInterval(function(){count++;bootQSE();if(count>=8){window.clearInterval(timer);}},600);
})();
</script>

<!-- [[SKAI_SAVED_TEMPLATES_PANEL]] Reuse a saved My Lotto Expert SKAI setup -->
<div id="skai-saved-templates-panel"
     style="margin:0 0 14px 0;border:1px solid rgba(28,102,255,0.22);border-radius:14px;background:#f8fbff;overflow:hidden;">
  <div style="padding:12px 16px 10px 16px;border-bottom:1px solid rgba(28,102,255,0.12);">
    <div style="font-size:0.75rem;font-weight:700;letter-spacing:.08em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#1C66FF;margin-bottom:4px;">
      Saved SKAI Settings
    </div>
    <div style="font-size:0.92rem;font-weight:700;color:#0A1A33;margin-bottom:2px;">
      Reuse a Previous SKAI Configuration
    </div>
    <p style="font-size:0.82rem;color:#475569;margin:0;line-height:1.5;">
      These are your saved SKAI configurations from My Lotto Expert for this lottery.
      Click <strong>Load</strong> to apply a saved setup to the controls below, then run SKAI using those exact settings.
    </p>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:10px 14px 12px 14px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:8px;">
    <?php foreach ($skaiSavedTemplates as $__tpl): ?>
    <?php
      // [[SKAI_LOAD_AND_RUN_DATA_ATTR]]
      // Store params in a data attribute rather than embedding JSON inline in onclick.
      // JSON_HEX_QUOT converts " to \u0022 which is invalid as a JS object-literal key
      // in inline event handlers, causing SyntaxError: missing ) after argument list.
      // htmlspecialchars with ENT_QUOTES safely encodes " as &quot; for the HTML attribute;
      // the browser restores it to " before passing the attribute value to JavaScript, so
      // JSON.parse() inside the onclick receives well-formed JSON and works correctly.
      $__tplParamsAttr = htmlspecialchars(
          json_encode($__tpl['params'], JSON_HEX_TAG | JSON_HEX_AMP | JSON_HEX_APOS | JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE),
          ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'
      );
      $__tplName     = htmlspecialchars($__tpl['name'],     ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8');
      $__tplProfile  = htmlspecialchars(ucfirst((string)$__tpl['profile']),  ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8');
      $__tplStrategy = htmlspecialchars((string)$__tpl['strategy'], ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8');
      $__tplDate     = htmlspecialchars($__tpl['date'],     ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8');
      $__tplBlend    = ($__tpl['blend'] !== null) ? (int)$__tpl['blend'] . '% AI' : '';
      $__tplEpochs   = ($__tpl['epochs'] !== null) ? (int)$__tpl['epochs'] . ' epochs' : '';
      $__tplMeta     = implode('  |  ', array_filter([$__tplProfile, $__tplStrategy, $__tplBlend, $__tplEpochs]));
    ?>
    <div style="display:flex;align-items:flex-start;justify-content:space-between;gap:10px;padding:10px 12px;border:1px solid rgba(28,102,255,0.14);border-radius:10px;background:#fff;">
      <div style="flex:1;min-width:0;">
        <div style="font-size:0.88rem;font-weight:700;color:#0A1A33;white-space:nowrap;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;"
             title="<?php echo $__tplName; ?>"><?php echo $__tplName; ?></div>
        <?php if ($__tplMeta !== ''): ?>
        <div style="font-size:0.78rem;color:#475569;margin-top:2px;"><?php echo $__tplMeta; ?></div>
        <?php endif; ?>
        <?php if ($__tplDate !== ''): ?>
        <div style="font-size:0.75rem;color:#94a3b8;margin-top:1px;">Saved <?php echo $__tplDate; ?></div>
        <?php endif; ?>
      </div>
      <!-- [[SKAI_LOAD_AND_RUN_BUTTONS]] Primary action: load settings then immediately run SKAI. -->
      <!-- Secondary action: load settings only so user can review/adjust before running.       -->
      <!-- params are stored in data-skai-params; onclick uses JSON.parse to avoid the           -->
      <!-- \u0022 SyntaxError that occurs when JSON_HEX_QUOT output is embedded inline.          -->
      <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:5px;flex-shrink:0;">
        <button type="button"
                data-skai-params="<?php echo $__tplParamsAttr; ?>"
                style="font-size:0.8rem;padding:5px 14px;white-space:nowrap;background:#1C66FF;color:#fff;border:1px solid #1553d4;border-radius:6px;cursor:pointer;font-weight:700;"
                onclick="var __sp;try{__sp=JSON.parse(this.getAttribute('data-skai-params'));}catch(__e){__sp=null;}if(__sp&&typeof skaiLoadAndRun==='function'){skaiLoadAndRun(__sp);}return false;"
                aria-label="Load settings and run SKAI: <?php echo $__tplName; ?>">
          Load and Run
        </button>
        <button type="button"
                data-skai-params="<?php echo $__tplParamsAttr; ?>"
                class="btn-outline"
                style="font-size:0.75rem;padding:3px 10px;white-space:nowrap;"
                onclick="var __sp;try{__sp=JSON.parse(this.getAttribute('data-skai-params'));}catch(__e){__sp=null;}if(__sp&&typeof skaiApplySavedTemplate==='function'){skaiApplySavedTemplate(__sp);}return false;"
                aria-label="Load settings only: <?php echo $__tplName; ?>">
          Load Only
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
    <?php endforeach; ?>
  </div>
  <div id="skai-saved-template-feedback"
       role="status" aria-live="polite"
       style="display:none;padding:6px 16px 10px 16px;font-size:0.82rem;font-weight:600;color:#065f46;"></div>
</div>
<?php endif; ?>

    <span id="skai-run-engine-profile-state"       class="skai-chip skai-chip--dot skai-chip--active">Profile: Balanced</span>

  </div>
